ar: عن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ سئل رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم أي العمل أفضل‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏إيمان بالله ورسوله‏"‏ قيل‏:‏ ثم ماذا‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏الجهاد في سبيل الله‏"‏ قيل‏:‏ ثم ماذا‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏حج مبرور‏"‏‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was asked, "Which deed is the best?" He (ﷺ) replied, "Faith in Allah and His Messenger." He (ﷺ) was asked, "What is next?" He (ﷺ) replied, "Jihad (holy fighting) in the Cause of Allah." Then he was asked: "What is after that (in goodness)?" He (ﷺ) replied, "Hajj Mabrur (which is accepted by Allah).".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was asked, "Which deed is the best?" He (ﷺ) replied, "Faith in Allah and His Messenger." He (ﷺ) was asked, "What is next?" He (ﷺ) replied, "Jihad (holy fighting) in the Cause of Allah." Then he was asked: "What is after that (in goodness)?" He (ﷺ) replied, "Hajj Mabrur (which is accepted by Allah).".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ was bertanya, "Which deed is the best?" He (ﷺ) menjawab, "Faith in Allah and His Messenger." He (ﷺ) was bertanya, "What is next?" He (ﷺ) menjawab, "Jihad (holy fighting) in the Cause of Allah." Kemudian he was bertanya: "What is setelah that (in goodness)?" He (ﷺ) menjawab, "haji Mabrur (which is accepted by Allah).".

ar: وعن ابن مسعود، رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قلت يا رسول الله أي العمل أحب إلى الله تعالى‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏الصلاة على وقتها‏"‏ قلت‏:‏ ثم أي‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏بر الوالدين‏"‏ قلت ثم أي‏:‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏الجهاد في سبيل الله‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: Ibn Mas'ud (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I asked the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ): "Which action is dearest to Allah?" He (ﷺ) replied, "Performing As-Salat (the prayer) at its earliest fixed time." I asked, "What is next (in goodness)?" He (ﷺ) said, "Kindness towards parents." I asked, "What is next (in goodness)?" He (ﷺ) said, "To participate in Jihad in the Cause of Allah.".
iwh_en: Ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that i asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: "Which action is dearest to Allah?" He (ﷺ) replied, "Performing As-Salat (the prayer) at its earliest fixed time." I asked, "What is next (in goodness)?" He (ﷺ) said, "Kindness towards parents." I asked, "What is next (in goodness)?" He (ﷺ) said, "To participate in Jihad in the Cause of Allah.".
iwh_id: Ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa i bertanya Rasulullah ﷺ: "Which action is dearest to Allah?" He (ﷺ) menjawab, "Performing As-Salat (the prayer) at its earliest fixed time." Saya bertanya, "What is next (in goodness)?" He (ﷺ) bersabda, "Kindness kepada parents." Saya bertanya, "What is next (in goodness)?" He (ﷺ) bersabda, "To participate in Jihad in the Cause of Allah.".

sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Verily! Setting out in the early morning or in the evening in order to fight in Allah's way is better than the world and what it contains.".

ar: وعن أبي سعيد الخدري رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ أتى رجل رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم فقال‏:‏ أي الناس أفضل‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏مؤمن يجاهد بنفسه وماله في سبيل الله‏"‏ قال‏:‏ ثم من‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏مؤمن في شعب من الشعاب يعبد الله ويدع الناس من شره‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A man came to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and said, "Who is the best among men?" He (ﷺ) replied, "A believer who strives in the way of Allah with his wealth and life." The man asked again, "Who is next to him (in excellence)?" He (ﷺ) said, "Next to him is a man who is engaged in worshipping his Rubb in a mountain valley, leaving the people secure from his mischief.".
iwh_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a man came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said, "Who is the best among men?" He (ﷺ) replied, "A believer who strives in the way of Allah with his wealth and life." The man asked again, "Who is next to him (in excellence)?" He (ﷺ) said, "Next to him is a man who is engaged in worshipping his Rubb in a mountain valley, leaving the people secure from his mischief.".
iwh_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa seorang laki-laki came to Rasulullah ﷺ dan bersabda, "Who is the best di antara men?" He (ﷺ) menjawab, "A believer who strives in the way of Allah with his wealth dan life." The man bertanya again, "Who is next to him (in excellence)?" He (ﷺ) bersabda, "Next to him is seorang laki-laki who is engaged in worshipping his Rubb in a mountain valley, leaving the orang-orang secure from his mischief.".

ar: وعن سهل بن سعد رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏رباط يوم في سبيل الله خير من الدنيا وما عليها، وموضع سوط أحدكم من الجنة خير من الدنيا وما عليها، والروحة يروحها العبد في سبيل الله تعالى، أو الغدوة خير من الدنيا وما عليها‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Sahl

sunnah_ref_en: Sahl bin Sa'd (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Observing Ribat (e.g., guarding the Islamic frontier for the sake of Allah) for a single day is far better than the world and all that it contains. A place in Jannah as small as the whip of your horse is far better than the world and all that it contains. An endeavour (fighting) in the Cause of Allah in the evening or in the morning is far better than the world and all that it contains.".
iwh_en: Sahl bin Sa'd (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Observing Ribat (e.g., guarding the Islamic frontier for the sake of Allah) for a single day is far better than the world and all that it contains. A place in Jannah as small as the whip of your horse is far better than the world and all that it contains. An endeavour (fighting) in the Cause of Allah in the evening or in the morning is far better than the world and all that it contains.".
iwh_id: Sahl bin Sa'd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Observing Ribat (e.g., guarding the Islamic frontier for the sake of Allah) for a single day is far better than dunia dan all that it contains. A place in Jannah as kecil as the whip of your horse is far better than dunia dan all that it contains. An endeavour (fighting) in the Cause of Allah in the evening or in the morning is far better than dunia dan all that it contains.".

ar:
وعن سلمان رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول‏:‏ ‏
"‏رباط يوم وليلة خير من صيام شهر وقيامه، وإن مات فيه أجري عليه عمله الذي كان يعمل، وأجري عليه رزقه، وأمن الفتان‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Salman (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) as saying, "Observing Ribat in the way of Allah for a day and a night is far better than observing Saum (fasting) for a whole month and standing in Salat (prayer) in all its nights. If a person dies (while performing this duty), he will go on receiving his reward for his meritorious deeds perpetually, and he will be saved from Al- Fattan.".
iwh_en: Salman (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that i heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ as saying, "Observing Ribat in the way of Allah for a day and a night is far better than observing Saum (fasting) for a whole month and standing in Salat (prayer) in all its nights. If a person dies (while performing this duty), he will go on receiving his reward for his meritorious deeds perpetually, and he will be saved from Al- Fattan.".
iwh_id: Salman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa i heard Rasulullah ﷺ as saying, "Observing Ribat in the way of Allah for a day dan a night is far better than observing Saum (fasting) for a whole month dan standing in Salat (prayer) in all its nights. If seseorang dies (while performing this duty), he will go on receiving his reward for his meritorious deeds perpetually, dan ia will be saved from Al- Fattan.".

ar: وعن فضالة بن عبيد رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏كل ميت يختم على عمله إلا المرابط في سبيل الله فإنه ينمى له عمله إلى يوم القيامة، ويؤمن فتنة القبر” ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن صحيح‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Fadalah bin 'Ubaid

sunnah_ref_en: Fadalah bin 'Ubaid (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "The actions of every dead person come to a halt with his death except the one who is on the frontier in Allah's way (i.e., observing Ribat). This latter's deeds will be made to go on increasing for him till the Day of Resurrection, and he will be secure from the trials in the grave.".
iwh_en: Fadalah bin 'Ubaid (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "The actions of every dead person come to a halt with his death except the one who is on the frontier in Allah's way (i.e., observing Ribat). This latter's deeds will be made to go on increasing for him till the Day of Resurrection, and he will be secure from the trials in the grave.".
iwh_id: Fadalah bin 'Ubaid (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "The actions of every dead person come to a halt with his death except the one who is on the frontier in Allah's way (i.e., observing Ribat). This latter's deeds will be membuat to go on increasing for him till the Hari Kebangkitan, dan ia will be secure from the trials in the grave.".

sunnah_ref_en: 'Uthman (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saying: "Spending a day on the frontier in Allah's way is better than one thousand days in any other place.".
iwh_en: 'Uthman (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying: "Spending a day on the frontier in Allah's way is better than one thousand days in any other place.".
iwh_id: 'Uthman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan: I heard Rasulullah ﷺ saying: "Spending a day on the frontier in Allah's way is better than one thousand days in any other place.".

ar:
وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏"‏تضمن الله لمن خرج في سبيله لا يخرجه إلا جهاد في سبيلي، وإيمان بي وتصديق برسلي فهو علي ضامن أن أدخله الجنة، أو أرجعه إلى منزله الذي خرج منه بما نال من أجر، أو غنيمة، والذي نفس محمد بيده ما من كلم يكلم في سبيل الله إلا جاء يوم القيامة كهيئته يوم كلم، لونه لون دم، وريحه ريح مسك، والذي نفس محمد بيده لولا أن يشق على المسلمين ما قعدت خلاف سرية تغزو في سبيل الله أبدا، ولكن لا أجد سعة فأحملهم ولا يجدون سعة عليهم أن يتخلفوا عني، والذي نفس محمد بيده لوددت أن أغزو في سبيل الله فأقتل، ثم أغزو فأقتل ثم أغزو فأقتل‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم وروى البخاري بعضه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏ 
‏(‏‏(‏‏"‏الكلم‏"‏ الجرح‏.‏‏)‏‏)‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Allah guarantees that he who goes out to fight in His way believing in Him and affirming the truth of His Messenger, will either be admitted to Jannah or will be brought back to his home (safely) from where he has set out, with whatever reward or share of booty he may have gained. By Him in Whose Hand Muhammad's soul is, if a person is wounded in the way of Allah, he will come on the Day of Resurrection with his wound in the same condition as it was on the day when he received it; its colour will be the colour of blood but its smell will be the smell of musk. By Him in Whose Hand Muhammad's soul is, if it were not to be too hard upon the Muslims, I would not lag behind any expedition to fight in the Cause of Allah, but I have neither abundant means to provide them conveyance (horses) nor all other Muslims have it, and it will be hard on them to remain behind when I go forth (for Jihad). By Him in Whose Hand Muhammad's soul is, I love to fight in the way of Allah and get killed, to fight again and get killed and to fight again and get killed.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah guarantees that he who goes out to fight in His way believing in Him and affirming the truth of His Messenger, will either be admitted to Jannah or will be brought back to his home (safely) from where he has set out, with whatever reward or share of booty he may have gained. By Him in Whose Hand Muhammad's soul is, if a person is wounded in the way of Allah, he will come on the Day of Resurrection with his wound in the same condition as it was on the day when he received it; its colour will be the colour of blood but its smell will be the smell of musk. By Him in Whose Hand Muhammad's soul is, if it were not to be too hard upon the Muslims, I would not lag behind any expedition to fight in the Cause of Allah, but I have neither abundant means to provide them conveyance (horses) nor all other Muslims have it, and it will be hard on them to remain behind when I go forth (for Jihad). By Him in Whose Hand Muhammad's soul is, I love to fight in the way of Allah and get killed, to fight again and get killed and to fight again and get killed.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "Allah guarantees that barangsiapa yang goes out to fight in His way believing in Him dan affirming the truth of His Messenger, will either be admitted to Jannah or will be membawa back to his home (safely) from where he has set out, with whatever reward or share of booty he may have gained. By Him in Whose Hand Muhammad's soul is, if seseorang is wounded di way of Allah, he will come on Hari Kebangkitan with his wound di same condition as it was di day ketika ia received it; its colour will be the colour of blood namun its smell will be the smell of musk. By Him in Whose Hand Muhammad's soul is, if it bukan to be too hard upon the Muslims, I tidak akan lag di belakang any expedition to fight di Cause of Allah, namun I have neither abundant means to provide them conveyance (horses) dan tidak all other Muslims have it, and it will be hard on them to remain di belakang when I go forth (for Jihad). By Him in Whose Hand Muhammad's soul is, I love to fight di way of Allah dan get killed, to fight again dan get killed dan to fight again dan get killed.".

ar: وعنه قال‏:‏ قال‏:‏ رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏ما من مكلوم يكلم في سبيل الله إلا جاء يوم القيامة، وكلمه يدمي اللون لون دم، والريح ريح مسك‏:‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Whoever is wounded while fighting in the way of Allah, will come on the Day of Resurrection with blood oozing from his wound having the colour of blood but with the fragrance of musk.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Whoever is wounded while fighting in the way of Allah, will come on the Day of Resurrection with blood oozing from his wound having the colour of blood but with the fragrance of musk.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Whoever is wounded while fighting in the way of Allah, will come di Hari Kebangkitan with blood oozing from his wound having the colour of blood namun with the fragrance of musk.".

ar:
وعن معاذ رضي الله عنه عن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏من قاتل في سبييل الله من رجل مسلم فواق ناقة وجبت له الجنة، ومن جرح جرحًا في سبيل الله أو نكب نكبة فإنها تجيء يوم القيامة كأغزر ما كانت‏:‏ لونها الزعفران، وريحها كالمسك‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Muadh (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Jannah becomes incumbent for a Muslim who fights for the Cause of Allah for a period as long as the time between two consecutive turns of milking a she-camel. He who receives a wound or a bruise in the Cause of Allah will appear on the Day of Resurrection as fresh as possible, its colour will be the colour of saffron and its fragrance will be that of musk.".
iwh_en: Muadh (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Prophet ﷺ said, "Jannah becomes incumbent for a Muslim who fights for the Cause of Allah for a period as long as the time between two consecutive turns of milking a she-camel. He who receives a wound or a bruise in the Cause of Allah will appear on the Day of Resurrection as fresh as possible, its colour will be the colour of saffron and its fragrance will be that of musk.".
iwh_id: Muadh (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata, "Jannah becomes incumbent for a Muslim who fights for the Cause of Allah for a period as panjang as the time antara two consecutive turns of milking a she-camel. Barangsiapa yang receives a wound or a bruise in the Cause of Allah will appear on Hari Kebangkitan as fresh as possible, its colour will be the colour of saffron dan its fragrance will be that of musk.".

ar: وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ مر رجل من أصحاب رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم بشعب فيه عيينة من ماء عذبة فأعجبته، فقال‏:‏ لو اعتزلت الناس فأقمت في هذا الشعب، ولن أفعل حتى استأذن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم فذكر ذلك لرسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم فقال‏:‏ ‏"‏لا تفعل فإن مقام أحدكم في سبيل الله أفضل من صلاته في بيته سبعين عامًا، ألا تحبون أن يغفر الله لكم ويدخلكم الجنة‏؟‏ اغزو في سبيل الله من قاتل في سبيل الله فواق ناقة وجبت له الجنة‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏‏"‏والفواق‏"‏‏:‏ ما بين الحلبتين‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: One of the Prophet's Companions came upon a valley containing a rivulet of fresh water and was delighted by it. He reflected: 'I wish to withdraw from people and settle in this valley; but I won't do so without the permission of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ).' This was mentioned to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and he said (to the man), "Do not do that, for when any of you remains in Allah's way, it is better for him than performing Salat (prayer) in his house for seventy years. Do you not wish that Allah should forgive you and admit you to Jannah? Fight in Allah's way, for he who fights in Allah's Cause as long as the time between two consecutive turns of milking a she-camel, will be surely admitted to Jannah.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that one of the Prophet ﷺ's Companions came upon a valley containing a rivulet of fresh water and was delighted by it. He reflected: 'I wish to withdraw from people and settle in this valley; but I won't do so without the permission of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.' This was mentioned to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and he said (to the man), "Do not do that, for when any of you remains in Allah's way, it is better for him than performing Salat (prayer) in his house for seventy years. Do you not wish that Allah should forgive you and admit you to Jannah? Fight in Allah's way, for he who fights in Allah's Cause as long as the time between two consecutive turns of milking a she-camel, will be surely admitted to Jannah.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa one of Nabi ﷺ's Companions came upon a valley containing a rivulet of fresh water dan was delighted by it. He reflected: 'I wish to withdraw from orang-orang dan settle in this valley; namun I won't do jadi tanpa the permission of Rasulullah ﷺ.' This was mentioned to Rasulullah ﷺ dan ia berkata (to the man), "Jangan do that, for when any of you remains in Allah's way, it is better for him than performing Salat (shalat) di rumahnya for seventy years. Do you not wish that Allah should forgive you dan admit you to Jannah? Fight in Allah's way, for barangsiapa yang fights in Allah's Cause as panjang as the time antara two consecutive turns of milking a she-camel, will be surely admitted to Jannah.".

ar: وعنه قال قيل يا رسول الله‏:‏ ما يعدل الجهاد في سبيل الله‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏لاتستطيعونه‏"‏ فأعادوا عليه مرتين أو ثلاثًا كل ذلك يقول‏:‏ ‏"‏لاتستطيعونه‏!‏‏"‏ ثم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏مثل المجاهد في سبيل الله كمثل الصائم القائم القانت بآيات الله لا يفتر‏:‏ من صلاة ولا صيام، حتى يرجع المجاهد في سبيل الله‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه، وهذا لفظ مسلم‏)‏‏)‏ ‏.‏ وفي رواية البخاري، أن رجلا قال‏:‏ يا رسول الله دلني على عمل يعدل الجهاد‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏لا أجده‏"‏ ثم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏هل تستطيع إذا خرج المجاهد أن تدخل مسجدك فتقوم ولا تفتر وتصوم ولا تفطر‏؟‏ فقال‏:‏ ومن يستطيع ذلك‏؟‏‏!‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعنه قال قيل يا رسول الله: ما يعدل الجهاد في سبيل الله؟ قال: "لاتستطيعونه" فأعادوا عليه مرتين أو ثلاثًا كل ذلك يقول: "لاتستطيعونه!" ثم قال: "مثل المجاهد في سبيل الله كمثل الصائم القائم القانت بآيات الله لا يفتر: من صلاة ولا صيام، حتى يرجع المجاهد في سبيل الله" ((متفق عليه، وهذا لفظ مسلم)) . وفي رواية البخاري، أن رجلا قال: يا رسول الله دلني على عمل يعدل الجهاد؟ قال: "لا أجده" ثم قال: "هل تستطيع إذا خرج المجاهد أن تدخل مسجدك فتقوم ولا تفتر وتصوم ولا تفطر؟ فقال: ومن يستطيع ذلك؟!.

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was asked: "What other good deed could be an equivalent of Jihad in the way of Allah?" He (ﷺ) replied, "You do not have the strength to do it." (The narrator said:) The question was repeated twice or thrice, but every time he (ﷺ) answered, "You do not have the strength to do it." Then he (ﷺ) said, "One who goes out for Jihad in the Cause of Allah is like a person who observes Saum (fasting), stands in Salat (prayer) constantly, recites the Ayat of the Qur'an and does not exhibit any lassitude in fasting and prayer until the participant of Jihad in the way of Allah returns.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was asked: "What other good deed could be an equivalent of Jihad in the way of Allah?" He (ﷺ) replied, "You do not have the strength to do it." (The narrator said:) The question was repeated twice or thrice, but every time he (ﷺ) answered, "You do not have the strength to do it." Then he (ﷺ) said, "One who goes out for Jihad in the Cause of Allah is like a person who observes Saum (fasting), stands in Salat (prayer) constantly, recites the Ayat of the Qur'an and does not exhibit any lassitude in fasting and prayer until the participant of Jihad in the way of Allah returns.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ was bertanya: "What other baik deed could be an equivalent of Jihad in the way of Allah?" He (ﷺ) menjawab, "You jangan have the strength to do it." (The narrator bersabda:) The question was repeated twice or thrice, namun every time he (ﷺ) menjawab, "You jangan have the strength to do it." Kemudian he (ﷺ) bersabda, "One who goes out for Jihad in the Cause of Allah is like seseorang who observes Saum (fasting), stands in Salat (prayer) constantly, recites the Ayat of the Qur'an dan does not exhibit any lassitude in fasting dan prayer hingga the participant of Jihad in the way of Allah returns.".

ar:
وعنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏من خير معاش الناس لهم رجل ممسك بعنان فرسه في سبيل الله، يطير على متنه كلما سمع هيعة، أو فزعة طار على متنه، يبتغي القتل أو الموت مظانه، أو رجل في غنيمة أو شعفة من هذه الشعف أو بطن واد من هذه الأودية يقيم الصلاة ويؤتي الزكاة، ويعبد ربه حتى يأتيه اليقين ليس من الناس إلا في خير‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "The best life is that of the man who holds his horse's rein in Allah's way and flies on its back to the places from whence he hears a war cry or the clatter of arms, seeking martyrdom or slaughter on the battlefield; or that of a person who goes to stay on the top of the hill or in a valley, and there he performs Salat (prayer), pays the Zakat and worships his Rubb till death overtakes him. He has no concern with the affairs of anyone except the doing of good.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "The best life is that of the man who holds his horse's rein in Allah's way and flies on its back to the places from whence he hears a war cry or the clatter of arms, seeking martyrdom or slaughter on the battlefield; or that of a person who goes to stay on the top of the hill or in a valley, and there he performs Salat (prayer), pays the Zakat and worships his Rubb till death overtakes him. He has no concern with the affairs of anyone except the doing of good.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "The best life is that of the man who holds his horse's rein in Allah's way dan flies on its back to the places from whence he hears a war cry or the clatter of arms, seeking martyrdom or slaughter on the battlefield; or that of seseorang who goes to stay on the top of the hill or in a valley, and there he performs Salat (prayer), pays the Zakat dan worships his Rubb till death overtakes him. He has no concern with the affairs of anyone except the doing of baik.".

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "In Jannah there are a hundred grades which Allah has prepared for those who fight in His Cause; and the distance between any two of those grades is like the distance between the heaven and the earth.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "In Jannah there are a hundred grades which Allah has prepared for those who fight in His Cause; and the distance between any two of those grades is like the distance between the heaven and the earth.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "In Jannah there are a hundred grades which Allah has prepared for those who fight in His Cause; dan the distance antara any two of those grades is like the distance antara the heaven dan the earth.".

ar: وعن أبي سعيد الخدري رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏من رضي بالله ربًا وبالإسلام دينًا، وبمحمد رسولا وجبت له الجنة‏"‏ فعجب لها أبو سعيد فقال أعدها علي يا رسول الله فأعادها عليه ثم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏وأخرى يرفع الله بها العبد مائة درجة في الجنة، ما بين كل درجتين، كما بين السماء والأرض‏"‏ قال‏:‏ وما هي يا رسول الله‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏الجهاد في سبيل الله، الجهاد في سبيل الله‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Sa’id Al-Khudri (rad)

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "If anyone is pleased with Allah as his Rubb, with Islam as his religion and with Muhammad (ﷺ) as (Allah's) Messenger, surely, he will be entitled to enter Jannah." Abu Sa'id was delighted with this and requested the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) to repeat it. He (ﷺ) repeated it again and then said, "There is also another act by which Allah will elevate the position of a (pious believing) slave in Jannah to a grade one hundred degrees higher. And the distance between any two grades is equal to the distance between heaven and earth." He asked the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) what it was and he ((ﷺ)) replied, "Jihad in the way of Allah; Jihad in the way of Allah.".
iwh_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "If anyone is pleased with Allah as his Rubb, with Islam as his religion and with Muhammad (ﷺ) as (Allah's) Messenger, surely, he will be entitled to enter Jannah." Abu Sa'id was delighted with this and requested the Messenger of Allah ﷺ to repeat it. He (ﷺ) repeated it again and then said, "There is also another act by which Allah will elevate the position of a (pious believing) slave in Jannah to a grade one hundred degrees higher. And the distance between any two grades is equal to the distance between heaven and earth." He asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ what it was and he ((ﷺ)) replied, "Jihad in the way of Allah; Jihad in the way of Allah.".
iwh_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "If anyone is pleased with Allah as his Rubb, with Islam as his religion dan with Muhammad (ﷺ) as (Allah's) Messenger, surely, he will be entitled to masuk ke Jannah." Abu Sa'id was delighted with this dan requested Rasulullah ﷺ to repeat it. He (ﷺ) repeated it again dan kemudian bersabda, "There is juga another act by which Allah will elevate the position of a (pious believing) slave in Jannah to a grade one hundred degrees higher. And the distance antara any two grades is equal to the distance antara heaven dan earth." Ia bertanya Rasulullah ﷺ what it was dan ia ((ﷺ)) menjawab, "Jihad in the way of Allah; Jihad in the way of Allah.".

ar: وعن أبي بكر بن أبي موسى الأشعري، قال‏:‏ سمعت أبي رضي الله عنه وهو بحضرة العدو، يقول قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏إن أبواب الجنة تحت ظلال السيوف‏"‏ فقام رجل رث الهيئة فقال يا أبا موسى أأنت سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول هذا‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ نعم، فرجع إلى أصحابه، فقال‏:‏ ‏"‏اقرأ عليكم السلام‏"‏ ثم كسر جفن سيفه فألقاه، ثم مشى بسيفه إلى العدو فضرب به حتى قتل‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Burda's father

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Bakr bin Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari reported: I heard my father saying in the presence of the enemy: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "The gates of Jannah are under the shades of the swords." A man with a shaggy appearance got up and said, "O Abu Musa! Did you hear the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say that in person?" Abu Musa replied in the affirmative; so he returned to his companions and said: "I tender you farewell greetings." Then he broke the scabbard of his sword and threw it away. He rushed towards the enemy with his sword and fought with it till he was martyred..
iwh_en: Abu Bakr bin Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that i heard my father saying in the presence of the enemy: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "The gates of Jannah are under the shades of the swords." A man with a shaggy appearance got up and said, "O Abu Musa! Did you hear the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say that in person?" Abu Musa replied in the affirmative; so he returned to his companions and said: "I tender you farewell greetings." Then he broke the scabbard of his sword and threw it away. He rushed towards the enemy with his sword and fought with it till he was martyred..
iwh_id: Abu Bakr bin Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa i heard my father saying in the presence of the enemy: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "The gates of Jannah are under the shades of the swords." A man with a shaggy appearance got up dan bersabda, "O Abu Musa! Did you hear Rasulullah ﷺ say that in person?" Abu Musa menjawab in the affirmative; jadi he returned to his companions dan bersabda: "I tender you farewell greetings." Kemudian he broke the scabbard of his sword dan threw it away. He rushed kepada the enemy with his sword dan fought with it till he was martyred..

"‏ما اغبرت قدما عبد في سبيل الله فتمسه النار‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_ar: أبي عبس عبد الرحمن بن جبير

sunnah_ref_en: Abu 'Abs 'Abdur-Rahman bin Jabr (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "It will not happen that the feet soiled with dust while (doing Jihad) in the way of Allah, will be touched by the fire (of Hell).".
iwh_en: Abu 'Abs 'Abdur-Rahman bin Jabr (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "It will not happen that the feet soiled with dust while (doing Jihad) in the way of Allah, will be touched by the fire (of Hell).".
iwh_id: Abu 'Abs 'Abdur-Rahman bin Jabr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "It tidak akan happen that the feet soiled with dust while (doing Jihad) in the way of Allah, will be touched by the fire (of Hell).".

ar:
وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏
"‏لا يلج النار رجل بكي من خشية الله حتى يعود اللبن في الضرع، ولا يجتمع على عبد غبار في سبيل الله ودخان جهنم‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن صحيح‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) said: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "No one who weeps out of Fear of Allah will enter Hell until milk recedes to the udder, and the dust endured while striving in the Cause of Allah and the smoke of Hell will never subsist together.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "No one who weeps out of Fear of Allah will enter Hell until milk recedes to the udder, and the dust endured while striving in the Cause of Allah and the smoke of Hell will never subsist together.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bersabda: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "No one who weeps out of Fear of Allah will masuk ke Hell hingga milk recedes to the udder, and the dust endured while striving in the Cause of Allah dan the smoke of Hell will never subsist together.".

ar: وعن ابن عباس، رضي الله عنهما قال‏:‏ سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول‏:‏ “عينان لا تمسهما النار عين بكت من خشية الله، وعين باتت تحرس في سبيل الله‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن‏.‏ ‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Abbas (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saying, "Two eyes will never be touched by the fire of Hell; an eye which weeps out of Fear of Allah and an eye which spends the night in guarding in the Cause of Allah  .".
iwh_en: Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that i heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying, "Two eyes will never be touched by the fire of Hell; an eye which weeps out of Fear of Allah and an eye which spends the night in guarding in the Cause of Allah  .".
iwh_id: Ibn 'Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa i heard Rasulullah ﷺ saying, "Two eyes will never be touched by the fire of Hell; an eye which weeps out of Fear of Allah dan an eye which spends the night in guarding in the Cause of Allah .".

ar:
وعن زيد بن خالد، رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏من جهز غازيًا في سبيل الله فقد غزا ومن خلف غازيا في أهله بخير فقد غزا‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: Zaid bin Khalid (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He who equips a Ghazi (fighter) in the way of Allah is as if he has taken part in the fighting himself; and he who looks after the dependants of a Ghazi in his absence, is as if he has taken part in the fighting himself.".
iwh_en: Zaid bin Khalid (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "He who equips a Ghazi (fighter) in the way of Allah is as if he has taken part in the fighting himself; and he who looks after the dependants of a Ghazi in his absence, is as if he has taken part in the fighting himself.".
iwh_id: Zaid bin Khalid (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He who equips a Ghazi (fighter) in the way of Allah is as if he has taken part in the fighting himself; dan ia who looks setelah the dependants of a Ghazi in his absence, is as if he has taken part in the fighting himself.".

ar:

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Umamah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "The best of charities is to provide canopy in the Cause of Allah, to pay wages to a servant in the way of Allah, and to provide a camel in the way of Allah (to be used by a Mujahid).".
iwh_en: Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "The best of charities is to provide canopy in the Cause of Allah, to pay wages to a servant in the way of Allah, and to provide a camel in the way of Allah (to be used by a Mujahid).".
iwh_id: Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "The best of charities is to provide canopy in the Cause of Allah, to pay wages to seorang hamba in the way of Allah, and to provide a camel in the way of Allah (to be menggunakan by a Mujahid).".

ar: وعن أنس رضي الله عنه أن فتى من أسلم قال‏:‏ يا رسول الله إني أريد الغزو وليس معي ما أتجهز به، قال‏:‏ ‏"‏ائت فلانًا، قد كان تجهز فمرض فأتاه فقال‏:‏ إن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقرئك السلام ويقول‏:‏ أعطني الذي تجهزت به قال‏:‏ يا فلانة أعطيه، الذي كنت تجهزت به ولاتحبسي منه شيئًا فوالله لا تحبسي منه شيئًا فيبارك لك  فيه ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A young man from the Aslam tribe said: "O Messenger of Allah! I would very much like to fight in the way of Allah but I do not have anything with which to equip myself for fighting." The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Go to so-and-so, for he had equipped himself (for fighting) but he fell ill." So, he (the young man) went to him and said: "The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) sends you his greetings and says that you should hand over to me the equipment that you have procured." The man said to his wife: "Give him the equipment which I have collected for myself and do not withhold anything from him. By Allah! Allah won't bless something you withheld (in this respect).".
iwh_en: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a young man from the Aslam tribe said: "O Messenger of Allah! I would very much like to fight in the way of Allah but I do not have anything with which to equip myself for fighting." the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Go to so-and-so, for he had equipped himself (for fighting) but he fell ill." So, he (the young man) went to him and said: "the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sends you his greetings and says that you should hand over to me the equipment that you have procured." The man said to his wife: "Give him the equipment which I have collected for myself and do not withhold anything from him. By Allah! Allah won't bless something you withheld (in this respect).".
iwh_id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa a muda man from the Aslam tribe bersabda: "O Rasulullah! I would very much like to fight in the way of Allah namun I jangan have anything with which to equip myself for fighting." Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Go to jadi-and-jadi, for he had equipped himself (for fighting) namun ia fell ill." Jadi, he (the muda man) went to him dan bersabda: "Rasulullah ﷺ sends you his greetings dan bersabda bahwa you should hand over to me the equipment that you have procured." The man bersabda to istrinya: "Give him the equipment which I have collected for myself dan jangan withhold anything from him. By Allah! Allah won't bless something you withheld (in this respect).".

ar:
وعن أبي سعيد الخدري رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم بعث إلى بني لحيان، فقال‏:‏ ‏
"‏لا ينبعث من كل رجلين أحدهما، والأجر بينهما‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Sa’id Al-Khudri (rad)

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) sent an expedition to Banu Lahyan and said, "One man from every two should join the fighting force, and the reward will be shared amongst them equally.".Another narration in Muslim is: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Let one of every two men go forth", and added: "Whoever stays behind (and looks well after the family and the property of those who have joined the expedition) will get half the reward of the warrior."
iwh_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent an expedition to Banu Lahyan and said, "One man from every two should join the fighting force, and the reward will be shared amongst them equally.".Another narration in Muslim is: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Let one of every two men go forth", and added: "Whoever stays behind (and looks well after the family and the property of those who have joined the expedition) will get half the reward of the warrior."
iwh_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ sent an expedition to Banu Lahyan dan bersabda, "One man from every two should join the fighting force, and the reward will be shared amongst them equally.".Another narration in Muslim is: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Let one of every two men go forth", and added: "Whoever stays di belakang (and looks well setelah the family dan the property of those who have joined the expedition) will get half the reward of the warrior."

ar: وعن البراء رضي الله عنه قال أتى النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم رجل مقنع بالحديد، فقال يا رسول الله أقاتل أو أسلم‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏أسلم، ثم قاتل‏"‏ فأسلم، ثم قاتل فقتل، فقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ عمل قليلا وأُجر كثيرًا‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه وهذا لفظ البخاري‏)‏‏)‏ ‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Al-Bara (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A man equipped with arms came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and asked: "O Messenger of Allah! Should I go and fight or should I embrace Islam first?" He (ﷺ) replied, "Enter in the fold of Islam and then fight." He embraced Islam and fought until he was killed. Thereupon the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He accepted Islam for a short time but was rewarded much.".
iwh_en: Al-Bara (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a man equipped with arms came to the Prophet ﷺ and asked: "O Messenger of Allah! Should I go and fight or should I embrace Islam first?" He (ﷺ) replied, "Enter in the fold of Islam and then fight." He embraced Islam and fought until he was killed. Thereupon the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "He accepted Islam for a short time but was rewarded much.".
iwh_id: Al-Bara (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa seorang laki-laki equipped with arms datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan bertanya: "O Rasulullah! Should I go dan fight or should I embrace Islam pertama?" He (ﷺ) menjawab, "Enter in the fold of Islam dan kemudian fight." He embraced Islam dan fought hingga he was killed. Thereupon Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "He accepted Islam for a pendek time namun was rewarded much.".

ar: وعن أنس رضي الله عنه أن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏ما أحد يدخل الجنة يحب أن يرجع إلى الدنيا وله ما على الأرض من شيء إلا الشهيد، يتمنى أن يرجع إلى الدنيا فيقتل عشر مرات لما يرى من الكرامة”

matn_ar: يتمنى أن يرجع إلى الدنيا فيقتل عشر مرات لما يرى من الكرامة”
matn_ar_clean: يتمنى أن يرجع إلى الدنيا فيقتل عشر مرات لما يرى من الكرامة”
sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "No one who has entered Jannah will desire to return to this world even if he should be given all that the world contains, except a martyr. For he will yearn that he should return to the world and be killed ten times on account of the dignity that he will experience by virtue of his martyrdom."Another narration is: "On account of the excellence and distinction, he will experience as a result of martyrdom.".
iwh_en: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Prophet ﷺ said, "No one who has entered Jannah will desire to return to this world even if he should be given all that the world contains, except a martyr. For he will yearn that he should return to the world and be killed ten times on account of the dignity that he will experience by virtue of his martyrdom."Another narration is: "On account of the excellence and distinction, he will experience as a result of martyrdom.".
iwh_id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata, "No one who has entered Jannah will desire to return to dunia ini even if he should be given all that dunia contains, except a martyr. For he will yearn that he should return to dunia dan be killed ten times on account of the dignity that he will experience by virtue of his martyrdom."Another narration is: "On account of the excellence dan distinction, he will experience as a result of martyrdom.".

isnad_chain.name_en: 'Amr bin Al-'As

sunnah_ref_en: 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'As (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Allah forgives every sin of a martyr, except his debt.".Another narration in Muslim is: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Being martyred in the Cause of Allah expiates for everything, except debt."
iwh_en: 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'As (may Allah be pleased with them) reported: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Allah forgives every sin of a martyr, except his debt.".Another narration in Muslim is: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Being martyred in the Cause of Allah expiates for everything, except debt."
iwh_id: 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'As (radhiyallahu 'anhum) meriwayatkan: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Allah forgives every sin of a martyr, except his debt.".Another narration in Muslim is: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Being martyred in the Cause of Allah expiates for everything, except debt."

ar: وعن أبي قتادة رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قام فيهم فذكر أن الجهاد في سبيل الله والإيمان بالله أفضل الأعمال فقام رجل فقال يا رسول الله أرأيت إن قتلت في سبيل الله أتكفر عني خطاياي‏؟‏ فقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏"‏نعم إن قتلت في سبيل الله وأنت صابر محتسب مقبل غير مدبر‏"‏ ثم قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏"‏كيف قلت‏؟‏‏"‏ قال‏:‏ أرأيت إن قتلت في سبيل الله أتكفر عني خطاياي‏؟‏ فقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏نعم وأنت صابر محتسب مقبل غير مدبر إلا الدين فإن جبريل عليه السلام قال لي ذلك‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Qatadah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) stood up among his Companions and said, "Jihad in the way of Allah and belief in Allah (with all His Attributes) are the most meritorious of actions." A man stood up and said: "O Messenger of Allah! Inform me if I am killed in the way of Allah, will my sins be blotted out?" The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Yes, in case you are killed in the way of Allah and you remained patient, hopeful of reward, and advancing forward without retracing back (i.e., while fighting)." Then he said, "What was your question?" He inquired again: "Inform me, if I am killed in the way of Allah, will all my sins be blotted out?" The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) replied, "If you remained patient, hopeful of reward and always fought without turning your back upon enemy, everything, except debt, will be forgiven. Jibril has told me this.".
iwh_en: Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood up among his Companions and said, "Jihad in the way of Allah and belief in Allah (with all His Attributes) are the most meritorious of actions." A man stood up and said: "O Messenger of Allah! Inform me if I am killed in the way of Allah, will my sins be blotted out?" the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Yes, in case you are killed in the way of Allah and you remained patient, hopeful of reward, and advancing forward without retracing back (i.e., while fighting)." Then he said, "What was your question?" He inquired again: "Inform me, if I am killed in the way of Allah, will all my sins be blotted out?" the Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied, "If you remained patient, hopeful of reward and always fought without turning your back upon enemy, everything, except debt, will be forgiven. Jibril has told me this.".
iwh_id: Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berdiri up di antara his Companions dan bersabda, "Jihad in the way of Allah dan belief in Allah (with all His Attributes) are the most meritorious of actions." A man berdiri up dan bersabda: "O Rasulullah! Inform me if I am killed in the way of Allah, will my sins be blotted out?" Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Yes, in case you are killed in the way of Allah dan you remained patient, hopeful of reward, and advancing forward tanpa retracing back (i.e., while fighting)." Kemudian he bersabda, "What was your question?" He inquired again: "Inform me, if I am killed in the way of Allah, will all my sins be blotted out?" Rasulullah ﷺ menjawab, "If you remained patient, hopeful of reward dan always fought tanpa turning your back upon enemy, everything, except debt, will be forgiven. Jibril has told me this.".

"‏ فألقى تمرات كن في يده ثم قاتل حتى قتل‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Jabir (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A man asked the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ): "Tell me where I will be if I am killed while fighting in the way of Allah?" He (ﷺ) replied, "In Jannah." The man threw away the few dates which he had in his hand, jumped into the battlefield and fought on till he was killed.
iwh_en: Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a man asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: "Tell me where I will be if I am killed while fighting in the way of Allah?" He (ﷺ) replied, "In Jannah." The man threw away the few dates which he had in his hand, jumped into the battlefield and fought on till he was killed.
iwh_id: Jabir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa seorang laki-laki bertanya Rasulullah ﷺ: "Tell me where I will be if I am killed while fighting in the way of Allah?" He (ﷺ) menjawab, "In surga." The man threw away the few dates which he had in his hand, jumped into the battlefield dan fought on till he was killed.

ar: وعن أنس رضي الله عنه قال انطلق رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم وأصحابه حتى سبقوا المشركين إلى بدر وجاء المشركون، فقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏"‏لا يقدمن أحد منكم إلى شيء حتى أكون أنا دونه‏"‏ فدنا المشركون، فقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏"‏قوموا إلى جنة عرضها السماوات والأرض‏"‏ قال‏:‏ يقول عمير بن الحمام الأنصاري رضي الله عنه‏:‏ يا رسول الله جنة عرضها السماوات والأرض‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏نعم‏"‏ قال‏:‏ بخ بخ‏!‏ فقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ “ما يحملك على قولك بخ بخ‏"‏ قال فإنك من أهلها فأخرج تمرات من قرنه فجعل يأكل منهن، ثم قال لئن أنا حييت حتى آكل تمراتي هذه إنها لحياة طويلة‏!‏ فرمى بما كان معه من التمر، ثم قاتلهم حتى قتل‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏ (4)

sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and his Companions reached Badr before the polytheists, and when they arrived, he (ﷺ) directed: "Let no one of you advance ahead of me." When the polytheists came near, the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Now stand up and proceed towards Jannah which is as wide as are the heavens and the earth." 'Umair bin Al-Humam (May Allah be pleased with him) asked: "Is Jannah as wide as are the heaven and the earth?" The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) replied in the affirmative. 'Umair remarked: "Great!" The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) asked him what had urged him to say so. He replied: "Nothing, O Messenger of Allah! But hope that I might become one of the inhabitants of Jannah." The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "You will definitely be among them." 'Umair then took some dates out of his quiver and began to eat them, but after a short time he said: "If I survive till I eat my dates, it will mean a long life." So he threw away the dates which he had with him and then fought with the enemy till he was killed..
iwh_en: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and his Companions reached Badr before the polytheists, and when they arrived, he (ﷺ) directed: "Let no one of you advance ahead of me." When the polytheists came near, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Now stand up and proceed towards Jannah which is as wide as are the heavens and the earth." 'Umair bin Al-Humam (may Allah be pleased with him) asked: "Is Jannah as wide as are the heaven and the earth?" the Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied in the affirmative. 'Umair remarked: "Great!" the Messenger of Allah ﷺ asked him what had urged him to say so. He replied: "Nothing, O Messenger of Allah! But hope that I might become one of the inhabitants of Jannah." the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "You will definitely be among them." 'Umair then took some dates out of his quiver and began to eat them, but after a short time he said: "If I survive till I eat my dates, it will mean a long life." So he threw away the dates which he had with him and then fought with the enemy till he was killed..
iwh_id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ dan para sahabatnya tiba di Badr sebelum the polytheists, and ketika mereka arrived, he (ﷺ) directed: "Let no one of you advance ahead of me." When the polytheists datang near, Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "Now berdiri up dan proceed kepada Jannah which is as wide as are langit dan bumi." 'Umair bin Al-Humam (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bertanya: "Is Jannah as wide as are the heaven dan bumi?" Rasulullah ﷺ menjawab di affirmative. 'Umair remarked: "Great!" Rasulullah ﷺ bertanya him what had urged him to say jadi. Ia menjawab: "Nothing, O Rasulullah! Namun hope that I might become one dari inhabitants of Jannah." Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "You will definitely be di antara them." 'Umair kemudian membawa some dates out of his quiver dan began to eat them, namun setelah a pendek time he berkata: "If I survive till I eat my dates, it will mean a panjang life." Jadi he threw away the dates which he had with him dan kemudian fought dengan enemy till he was killed..

وعنه قال‏:‏ جاء ناس إلى النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم أن ابعث معنا رجالا يعلمونا القرآن والسنة فبعث إليهم سبعين رجلا من الأنصار يقال لهم‏:‏ القراء، فيهم خالي حرام، يقرءون القرآن ويتدارسونه بالليل يتعلمون، وكانوا بالنهار يجيئون بالماء فيضعونه في المسجد، ويحتطبون فيبيعونه، ويشترون به الطعام لأهل الصفة، وللفقراء فبعثهم صلى الله عليه وسلم فعرضوا لهم فقتلوهم قبل أن يبلغوا المكان، فقالوا‏:‏ اللهم بلغ عنا نبينا أن قد لقيناك فرضينا عنك ورضيت عنا، وأتى رجل حراما خال أنس من خلف فطعنه برمح حتى أنفذه، فقال حرام‏:‏ فزت ورب الكعبة، فقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏
"‏إن إخوانكم قد قتلوا وإنهم قالوا‏:‏ اللهم بلغ عنا نبينا أنا قد لقيناك فرضينا عنك ورضيت عنا‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه وهذا لفظ مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

"إن إخوانكم قد قتلوا وإنهم قالوا: اللهم بلغ عنا نبينا أنا قد لقيناك فرضينا عنك ورضيت عنا" ((متفق عليه وهذا لفظ مسلم)).

قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم 
"إن إخوانكم قد قتلوا وإنهم قالوا: اللهم بلغ عنا نبينا أنا قد لقيناك فرضينا عنك ورضيت عنا" ((متفق عليه وهذا لفظ مسلم)).
sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: Some people came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and said to him: "Send with us some men who may teach us the Qur'an and the Sunnah." He (ﷺ) sent seventy men from the Ansar. They were called Al-Qurra' (the reciters) and among them was my maternal uncle, Haram. They used to recite the Qur'an, ponder over its meaning and learn (its wisdom) at night. In the day, they used to bring water and pour it in pitchers in the mosque, then they would collect wood and sell it; and with the sale proceeds, they would buy food for the people of As-Suffah and the needy. The Prophet (ﷺ) sent the reciters with these people but these (treacherous people) fell upon them and killed them before they reached their destination. (While dying) they supplicated: "O Allah convey from us the news to our Prophet that we have met You (in a way), that we are pleased with You and You are pleased with us." (The narrator said:) A man attacked Haram from behind and smote him with a spear which pierced him. Whereupon Haram said: "By the Rubb of Ka'bah, I have met with success. The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said to his Companions, "Your brethren have been slain and they were saying: "O Allah! Convey from us to our Prophet the news that we have met You (in a way) that we are pleased with You and You are pleased with us.".
iwh_en: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that some people came to the Prophet ﷺ and said to him: "Send with us some men who may teach us the Qur'an and the Sunnah." He (ﷺ) sent seventy men from the Ansar. They were called Al-Qurra' (the reciters) and among them was my maternal uncle, Haram. They used to recite the Qur'an, ponder over its meaning and learn (its wisdom) at night. In the day, they used to bring water and pour it in pitchers in the mosque, then they would collect wood and sell it; and with the sale proceeds, they would buy food for the people of As-Suffah and the needy. the Prophet ﷺ sent the reciters with these people but these (treacherous people) fell upon them and killed them before they reached their destination. (While dying) they supplicated: "O Allah convey from us the news to our Prophet that we have met You (in a way), that we are pleased with You and You are pleased with us." (The narrator said:) A man attacked Haram from behind and smote him with a spear which pierced him. Whereupon Haram said: "By the Rubb of Ka'bah, I have met with success. the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to his Companions, "Your brethren have been slain and they were saying: "O Allah! Convey from us to our Prophet the news that we have met You (in a way) that we are pleased with You and You are pleased with us.".
iwh_id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa some orang-orang datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan bersabda kepadanya: "Send bersama kami some men who may mengajarkan kami Qur'an dan the Sunnah." He (ﷺ) sent seventy men from the Ansar. They were called Al-Qurra' (the reciters) and di antara them was paman dari pihak ibuku, Haram. They biasa membaca the Qur'an, ponder over its meaning dan learn (its wisdom) at night. In the day, they biasa bring water dan menuangkan it in pitchers in the masjid, kemudian they would collect wood dan sell it; dan with the sale proceeds, they would buy food for the orang-orang of As-Suffah dan the needy. Nabi ﷺ sent the reciters with these orang-orang namun these (treacherous orang-orang) fell di atasnya dan killed them sebelum they tiba di their destination. (While dying) they supplicated: "Ya Allah convey from us the news to our Prophet that we have met You (in a way), that we are pleased with You and You are pleased bersama kami." (The narrator berkata:) A man attacked Haram from di belakang dan smote him with a spear which pierced him. Whereupon Haram berkata: "By the Rubb of Ka'bah, I have met with success. Rasulullah ﷺ berkata to para sahabatnya, "Your brethren have been slain dan mereka were saying: "Ya Allah! Convey from us to our Prophet the news that we have met You (in a way) that we are pleased with You and You are pleased bersama kami.".

ar: وعنه قال‏:‏ غاب عمي أنس بن النضر رضي الله عنه عن قتال بدر، فقال‏:‏ يا رسول الله غبت عن أول قتال قاتلت الشركين لئن الله أشهدني قتال المشركين ليرين الله ما أصنع‏.‏ فلما كان يوم أحد انكشف المسلمون، فقال اللهم إني اعتذر إليك مما صنع هؤلاء - يعني الصحابة- وأبرأ إليك مما صنع هؤلاء يعني المشركين- ثم تقدم فاستقبله سعد بن معاذ فقال‏:‏ يا سعد بن معاذ الجنة ورب النضر، إني أجد ريحها من دون أحد‏!‏ قال سعد‏:‏ فما استطعت يا رسول الله ما صنع‏!‏ قال أنس‏:‏ فوجدنا به بضعًا وثمانين ضربة بالسيف، أو طعنة برمح أو رمية بسهم، ووجدناه قد قتل ومثل به المشركون، فما عرفه أحد إلا أخته ببنانه، قال أنس‏:‏ كنا نرى -أو نظن- أن هذه الآية نزلت فيه وفي أشباهه‏:‏ ‏{‏من المؤمنين رجال صدقوا ما عاهدوا الله عليه فمنهم من قضى نحبه‏}‏ إلى آخره ‏(‏‏(‏الأحزاب 23‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏ وقد سبق في باب المجاهدة‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: My uncle Anas bin An- Nadr (May Allah be pleased with him) was absent from the battle of Badr. He said: "O Messenger of Allah! I was absent from the first battle you fought against the pagans. (By Allah!) if Allah gives me a chance to fight against the pagans, no doubt, Allah will see how (bravely) I will fight." On the Day of Uhud, when the Muslims turned their backs and fled, he said, "O Allah! I apologize to You for what these (i.e., his companions) have done, and I denounce what these (i.e., the pagans) have done." Then he advanced and Sa'd bin Mu'adh met him. He said: "O Sa'd bin Mu'adh! By the Rubb of An-Nadr, Jannah! I am smelling its aroma coming from before (the mountain of) Uhud," Later on, Sa'd said: "O Messenger of Allah! I cannot achieve or do what he (i.e., Anas bin An-Nadr) did. We found more than eighty wounds by swords, spears and arrows on his body. We found him dead and his body was mutilated so badly that none except his sister could recognise him by his finger." We used to think that the following Ayah was revealed concerning him and other men of his sort: "Among the believers are men who have been true to their covenant with Allah (i.e., they have gone out for Jihad, and showed not their backs to the disbelievers), of them some have fulfilled their obligations (i.e., have been martyred)." (33:23).

(33:23).
iwh_id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa my uncle Anas bin An- Nadr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) was absent from the battle of Badr. He bersabda: "O Rasulullah! I was absent from the pertama battle you fought terhadap the pagans. (By Allah!) if Allah gives me a chance to fight terhadap the pagans, no doubt, Allah will see how (bravely) I will fight." On the Day of Uhud, when the Muslims turned their backs dan fled, he bersabda, "Ya Allah! I apologize to You for what these (i.e., his companions) have done, dan aku denounce what these (i.e., the pagans) have done." Kemudian he advanced and Sa'd bin Mu'adh met him. He bersabda: "O Sa'd bin Mu'adh! By the Rubb of An-Nadr, Jannah! I am smelling its aroma coming from sebelum (the mountain of) Uhud," Later on, Sa'd bersabda: "O Rasulullah! I cannot achieve or do what he (i.e., Anas bin An-Nadr) did. We menemukan more than eighty wounds by swords, spears dan arrows on his body. We menemukan him dead dan his body was mutilated jadi badly that none except saudara perempuannya could recognise him by his finger." We biasa berpikir that the following Ayah was revealed concerning him dan other men of his sort: "Among the believers are men who have been true to their covenant with Allah (i.e., they have gone out for Jihad, and menunjukkan not their backs to the disbelievers), of them some have fulfilled their obligations (i.e., have been martyred)." (33:23).

ar: وعن سمرة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ “رأيت الليلة رجلين أتياني فصعدا بي الشجرة وأدخلاني دارًا هي أحسن وأفضل، لم أر قط أحسن منها، قال أما هذه الدار فدار الشهداء‏"‏ (5)

sunnah_ref_en: Samurah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Last night two men (angels) came to me (in a dream) and made me ascend a tree and then admitted me into a nice and excellent house, the like of which I have never seen before. One of them said: 'This house is the house of martyrs'.".
iwh_en: Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Last night two men (angels) came to me (in a dream) and made me ascend a tree and then admitted me into a nice and excellent house, the like of which I have never seen before. One of them said: 'This house is the house of martyrs'.".
iwh_id: Samurah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Last night two men (angels) came to me (in a dream) and membuat me ascend a tree dan kemudian admitted me into a nice dan excellent house, the like of which I have never seen sebelum. One of them bersabda: 'This house is the house of martyrs'.".

ar:
وعن أنس رضي الله عنه أن أم الربيع بنت البراء وهي أم حارثة بن سراقة أتت النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم فقالت‏:‏ يا رسول الله ألا تحدثني عن حارثة‏.‏ وكان قتل يوم بدر، فإن كان في الجنة صبرت، وإن كان غير ذلك اجتهدت عليه في البكاء، فقال‏:‏ ‏
"‏يا أم حارثة إنها جنان في الجنة، وإن ابنك أصاب الفردوس الأعلى‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: Umm Ar-Rubaiy'i bint Al-Bara', who was the mother of Harithah bin Suraqah, came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and said: "O Messenger of Allah! Will you not tell me about Harithah? (He was killed in the battle of Badr). If he is in Jannah I shall show endurance, but if he has met another fate, I may exert myself in weeping for him." He (ﷺ) replied, "O mother of Harithah, in in the gardens of Jannah there are many ranks, and your son has attained Al-Firdaus, the highest.".
iwh_en: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that umm Ar-Rubaiy'i bint Al-Bara', who was the mother of Harithah bin Suraqah, came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: "O Messenger of Allah! Will you not tell me about Harithah? (He was killed in the battle of Badr). If he is in Jannah I shall show endurance, but if he has met another fate, I may exert myself in weeping for him." He (ﷺ) replied, "O mother of Harithah, in in the gardens of Jannah there are many ranks, and your son has attained Al-Firdaus, the highest.".
iwh_id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa umm Ar-Rubaiy'i bint Al-Bara', who was the mother of Harithah bin Suraqah, datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: "O Rasulullah! Will you not tell me tentang Harithah? (He was killed in the battle of Badr). If he is in Jannah I shall menunjukkan endurance, namun if he has met another fate, I may exert myself in weeping for him." He (ﷺ) menjawab, "O mother of Harithah, in in the gardens of Jannah there are many ranks, and your son has attained Al-Firdaus, the highest.".

ar: وعن جابر بن عبد الله رضي الله عنهما قال‏:‏ جيء بأبي إلى النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم قد مثل به فوضع بين يديه، فذهبت أكشف عن وجهه فنهاني قوم فقال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏"‏ما زالت الملائكة تظله بأجنحتها” ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Jabir bin 'Abdullah (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The dead body of my father, who was mutilated by the enemy, was brought and placed before the Prophet (ﷺ). I got up to uncover his face but the people stopped me, and the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The angels continue to cover him with their wings.".
iwh_en: Jabir bin 'Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the dead body of my father, who was mutilated by the enemy, was brought and placed before the Prophet ﷺ. I got up to uncover his face but the people stopped me, and the Prophet ﷺ said, "The angels continue to cover him with their wings.".
iwh_id: Jabir bin 'Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa the dead body of my father, who was mutilated by the enemy, was membawa dan placed sebelum Nabi ﷺ. I got up to uncover his face namun the orang-orang berhenti me, and Nabi ﷺ berkata, "The angels continue to cover him with their wings.".

ar:
وعن سهل بن حنيف رضي الله عنه أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال‏:‏ ‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "The martyr experiences no more pain in being slain than one of you experiences from the stinging of an ant.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "The martyr experiences no more pain in being slain than one of you experiences from the stinging of an ant.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "The martyr experiences no more pain in being slain than one of you experiences from the stinging of an ant.".

ar: وعن عبد الله بن أبي أوفى رضي الله عنهما أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم في بعض أيامه التي لقي فيها العدو انتظر حتى مالت الشمس، ثم قام في الناس فقال‏:‏ ‏"‏ أيها الناس، لا تتمنوا لقاء العدو، وسلو الله العافية، فإذا لقيتموه فاصبروا، واعلموا أن الجنة تحت ظلال السيوف‏"‏ ثم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏اللهم منزل الكتاب ومجري السحاب، وهازم الأحزاب اهزمهم وانصرنا عليهم‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Ibn Abi 'Aufa

sunnah_ref_en: 'Abdullah bin Abu Aufa (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: On one occasion the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was confronting the enemy. He waited until the sun had declined. Then he stood up to address the people and said, "O people! Do not wish for an encounter with the enemy. Pray to Allah to grant you safety; (but) when you encounter them, show patience, and know that Jannah is under the shades of the swords." Then he (ﷺ) said: "Allahumma munzilal-kitab, wa mujriyas- sahab, wa hazimal-Ahzab, ihzimhum wansurna alaihim (O Allah, Revealer of the Book, Disperser of the clouds, Defeater of the Confederates, put our enemy to rout and support us against them).".
iwh_en: 'Abdullah bin Abu Aufa (may Allah be pleased with them) reported: On one occasion the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was confronting the enemy. He waited until the sun had declined. Then he stood up to address the people and said, "O people! Do not wish for an encounter with the enemy. Pray to Allah to grant you safety; (but) when you encounter them, show patience, and know that Jannah is under the shades of the swords." Then he (ﷺ) said: "Allahumma munzilal-kitab, wa mujriyas- sahab, wa hazimal-Ahzab, ihzimhum wansurna alaihim (O Allah, Revealer of the Book, Disperser of the clouds, Defeater of the Confederates, put our enemy to rout and support us against them).".
iwh_id: 'Abdullah bin Abu Aufa (radhiyallahu 'anhum) meriwayatkan: On one occasion Rasulullah ﷺ was confronting the enemy. He waited hingga the sun had declined. Kemudian he berdiri up to address the orang-orang dan bersabda, "O orang-orang! Jangan wish for an encounter with the enemy. Pray to Allah to grant you safety; (namun) when you encounter them, menunjukkan patience, and mengetahui that Jannah is under the shades of the swords." Kemudian he (ﷺ) bersabda: "Allahumma munzilal-kitab, wa mujriyas- sahab, wa hazimal-Ahzab, ihzimhum wansurna alaihim (Ya Allah, Revealer of the Book, Disperser of the clouds, Defeater of the Confederates, meletakkan our enemy to rout dan support us terhadap them).".

ar:

"‏ثنتان لا تردان، أو قلما تردان‏:‏ الدعاء عند النداء وعند البأس حين يلحم بعضهم بعضًا‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود بإسناد صحيح‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Sahl

sunnah_ref_en: Sahl bin Sa'd (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Supplications at two times are never turned down (or said, "Are seldom turned down"), a supplication after the Adhan has been proclaimed, and a supplication during the battle combating the enemy.".
iwh_en: Sahl bin Sa'd (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Supplications at two times are never turned down (or said, "Are seldom turned down"), a supplication after the Adhan has been proclaimed, and a supplication during the battle combating the enemy.".
iwh_id: Sahl bin Sa'd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Supplications at two times are never turned down (or bersabda, "Are seldom turned down"), a supplication setelah the Adhan has been proclaimed, and a supplication selama the battle combating the enemy.".

ar:
وعن أنس رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ كان رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم إذا غزا قال‏:‏ ‏
"‏اللهم أنت عضدي ونصيري، بك أحول وبك أجول وبك أصول، وبك أقاتل‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود، والترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: Whenever the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) set out to participate in Jihad, he would supplicate: "Allahumma Anta 'adudi wa nasiri, bika ahulu, wa bika asulu, wa bika uqatilu (O Allah, You are my Supporter and my Helper. With Your help I get strength, and with Your help I bounce upon the enemy and defeat it, and with Your help I fight).".
iwh_en: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that whenever the Messenger of Allah ﷺ set out to participate in Jihad, he would supplicate: "Allahumma Anta 'adudi wa nasiri, bika ahulu, wa bika asulu, wa bika uqatilu (O Allah, You are my Supporter and my Helper. With Your help I get strength, and with Your help I bounce upon the enemy and defeat it, and with Your help I fight).".
iwh_id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa setiap kali Rasulullah ﷺ set out to participate in Jihad, ia supplicate: "Allahumma Anta 'adudi wa nasiri, bika ahulu, wa bika asulu, wa bika uqatilu (Ya Allah, You are my Supporter dan my Helper. With Your help I get strength, and with Your help I bounce upon the enemy dan defeat it, and with Your help I fight).".

ar: وعن أبي موسى، رضي الله عنه أن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم كان إذا خاف قومًا قال‏:‏ اللهم إنا نجعلك في نحورهم، ونعوذ بك من شرورهم‏"‏‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود بإسناد صحيح‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعن أبي موسى، رضي الله عنه أن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم كان إذا خاف قومًا قال: اللهم إنا نجعلك في نحورهم، ونعوذ بك من شرورهم". ((رواه أبو داود بإسناد صحيح)).
isnad_ar: وعن أبي موسى، رضي الله عنه

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: When the Prophet (ﷺ) had any fear of an enemy, he used to supplicate: "Allahumma inna naj'aluka fi nuhurihim, wa na'udhu bika min shururihim (O Allah! We put You in front of them, and we seek refuge in You from their evils).".
iwh_en: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that when the Prophet ﷺ had any fear of an enemy, he used to supplicate: "Allahumma inna naj'aluka fi nuhurihim, wa na'udhu bika min shururihim (O Allah! We put You in front of them, and we seek refuge in You from their evils).".
iwh_id: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa ketika Nabi ﷺ had any fear of an enemy, he biasa supplicate: "Allahumma inna naj'aluka fi nuhurihim, wa na'udhu bika min shururihim (Ya Allah! We meletakkan You in front of them, dan kami berlindung in You from their evils).".

ar:

"‏الخيل معقود في نواصيها الخير إلى يوم القيامة‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: Ibn 'Umar (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "There is goodness in the forelocks of horses till the Day of Resurrection."
iwh_en: Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "There is goodness in the forelocks of horses till the Day of Resurrection."
iwh_id: Ibn 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "There is goodness in the forelocks of horses till the Hari Kebangkitan."

sunnah_ref_en: 'Urwah Al-Bariqi (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Goodness is tied to the foreheads of horses until the Day of Resurrection, i.e., reward (in the Hereafter) and spoils.".
iwh_en: 'Urwah Al-Bariqi (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: the Prophet ﷺ said, "Goodness is tied to the foreheads of horses until the Day of Resurrection, i.e., reward (in the Hereafter) and spoils.".

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He who keeps a horse for Jihad purposes, having faith in Allah and relying on His Promise, will find that its fodder, drink, droppings and urine will all be credited to him in his Scales on the Day of Resurrection.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "He who keeps a horse for Jihad purposes, having faith in Allah and relying on His Promise, will find that its fodder, drink, droppings and urine will all be credited to him in his Scales on the Day of Resurrection.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He who keeps a horse for Jihad purposes, having faith in Allah dan relying on His Promise, will menemukan that its fodder, drink, droppings dan urine will all be credited to him in his Scales di Hari Kebangkitan.".

ar:
وعن أبي مسعود رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ جار رجل إلى النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم بناقة مخطومة فقال‏:‏ هذه في سبيل الله، فقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏:‏ ‏
"‏لك بها يوم القيامة سبعمائة ناقة كلها مخطومة‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

matn_ar_clean:

"لك بها يوم القيامة سبعمائة ناقة كلها مخطومة" ((رواه مسلم)).
sunnah_ref_en: Abu Mas'ud (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A man came to the Prophet (ﷺ) with a she-camel wearing a nose-string and said: "This is (a gift) in the Cause of Allah." The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) replied, "You will have in return for it on the Day of Resurrection seven hundred she-camels and every one of them will be wearing a nose-string.".
iwh_en: Abu Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ with a she-camel wearing a nose-string and said: "This is (a gift) in the Cause of Allah." the Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied, "You will have in return for it on the Day of Resurrection seven hundred she-camels and every one of them will be wearing a nose-string.".
iwh_id: Abu Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa seorang laki-laki datang kepada Nabi ﷺ with a she-camel mengenakan a nose-string dan berkata: "This is (a gift) in the Cause of Allah." Rasulullah ﷺ menjawab, "You will have in return for it on Hari Kebangkitan seven hundred she-camels dan every one of them will be mengenakan a nose-string.".

وعن أبي حماد- ويقال‏:‏ أبو سعاد، ويقال‏:‏ أبو أسد، ويقال‏:‏ أبو عامر، ويقال‏:‏ أبو عمرو، ويقال‏:‏ أبو الأسود، ويقال‏:‏ أبو عبس- عقبة بن عامر الجهني، رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم وهو على المنبر يقول‏:‏ “وأعدوا لهم ما استطعتم من قوة ألا إن القوة الرمي، ألا إن القوة الرمي، ألا إن القوة الرمي‏"‏‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏ 
وعنه قال‏:‏ سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول‏:‏ ‏"‏ستفتح عليكم أرضون ويكفيكم الله فلا يعجز أحدكم أن يلهو بأسهمه‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: 'Uqbah bin 'Amir Al-Juhani (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saying from the pulpit, "Prepare to meet them (the enemy) with as much strength as you can afford. Verily! Strength is in archery, strength is in archery, strength is in archery.".
iwh_en: 'Uqbah bin 'Amir Al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying from the pulpit, "Prepare to meet them (the enemy) with as much strength as you can afford. Verily! Strength is in archery, strength is in archery, strength is in archery.".
iwh_id: 'Uqbah bin 'Amir Al-Juhani (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan: I heard Rasulullah ﷺ saying from the pulpit, "Prepare to meet them (the enemy) with as much strength as you can afford. Verily! Strength is in archery, strength is in archery, strength is in archery.".

iwh_en: 'Uqbah bin 'Amir Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying, "Lands shall be laid open to you, and Allah will suffice you (against your enemies), but none of you should neglect practicing his skill in archery.".

ar:
وعنه أنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏
"‏من عُلم الرمي ثم تركه فليس منا أو فقد عصى‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean:

"من عُلم الرمي ثم تركه فليس منا أو فقد عصى" ((رواه مسلم)).
isnad_ar: وعنه أنه قال:

matn_ar_clean:
قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم : 
"من علم الرمي ثم تركه فليس منا أو فقد عصى" ((رواه مسلم)).
sunnah_ref_en: 'Uqbah bin 'Amir Al-Juhani (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He who learnt archery and then neglected it, is not from us." (Or said,) "He has been guilty of disobedience (to Messenger of Allah).".
iwh_en: 'Uqbah bin 'Amir Al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "He who learnt archery and then neglected it, is not from us." (Or said,) "He has been guilty of disobedience (to Messenger of Allah).".
iwh_id: 'Uqbah bin 'Amir Al-Juhani (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He who learnt archery dan kemudian neglected it, bukan from us." (Or bersabda,) "He has been guilty of disobedience (to Rasulullah).".

ar: وعنه، رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول‏:‏ ‏"‏إن الله يدخل بالسهم الواحد ثلاثة نفر الجنة‏:‏ صانعه يحتسب في صنعته الخير، والرامي به ومنبله‏.‏ وارموا واركبوا، وأن ترموا أحب إلي من أن تركبوا‏.‏ ومن ترك الرمي بعد ما علمه رغبة عنه فإنها نعمة تركها‏"‏ أو قال‏:‏ ‏"‏كفرها‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعنه، رضي الله عنه قال: سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول: "إن الله يدخل بالسهم الواحد ثلاثة نفر الجنة: صانعه يحتسب في صنعته الخير، والرامي به ومنبله. وارموا واركبوا، وأن ترموا أحب إلي من أن تركبوا. ومن ترك الرمي بعد ما علمه رغبة عنه فإنها نعمة تركها" أو قال: "كفرها" ((رواه أبو داود)).
