sunnah_ref_en: 'Uqbah bin 'Amir Al-Juhani (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saying, "Allah will admit three persons to Jannah for one arrow; the maker who has a good motive in making it, the one who shoots it, and the one who hands it up for shooting. So shoot and ride, but I like your shooting (more) than your riding. He who gives up archery after becoming adept in it for lack of interest, neglects a (great) blessing." (Or said,) "One who does so is ungrateful.".
iwh_en: 'Uqbah bin 'Amir Al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying, "Allah will admit three persons to Jannah for one arrow; the maker who has a good motive in making it, the one who shoots it, and the one who hands it up for shooting. So shoot and ride, but I like your shooting (more) than your riding. He who gives up archery after becoming adept in it for lack of interest, neglects a (great) blessing." (Or said,) "One who does so is ungrateful.".
iwh_id: 'Uqbah bin 'Amir Al-Juhani (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan: I heard Rasulullah ﷺ saying, "Allah will admit three orang-orang to Jannah for one arrow; the maker who has a baik motive in making it, the one who shoots it, and the one who hands it up for shooting. Jadi shoot dan ride, namun I like your shooting (more) than your riding. He who gives up archery setelah becoming adept in it for lack of interest, neglects a (besar) blessing." (Or bersabda,) "One who does jadi is ungrateful.".

ar:
وعن سلمه بن الأكوع، رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ مر النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم على نفر ينتضلون، فقال‏:‏ ‏
"‏ارموا بني إسماعيل فإن أباكم كان راميًا‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Salamah bin Al-Akwa (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) happened to pass by a group of people who were having a shooting match. (Upon seeing them, he (ﷺ)) said, "Shoot, O sons of (Prophet) Isma'il, for your father was an archer.".
iwh_en: Salamah bin Al-Akwa (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Prophet ﷺ happened to pass by a group of people who were having a shooting match. (Upon seeing them, he (ﷺ)) said, "Shoot, O sons of (Prophet) Isma'il, for your father was an archer.".
iwh_id: Salamah bin Al-Akwa (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ happened to pass by a group of orang-orang who were having a shooting match. (Upon seeing them, he (ﷺ)) berkata, "Shoot, O sons of (Prophet) Isma'il, for your father was an archer.".

ar: وعن عمرو بن عبسة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول‏:‏ من رمى بسهم في سبيل الله فهو له عدل محررة‏:‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود، والترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن صحيح‏.‏ ‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: 'Amr bin 'Abasah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saying: "He who shoots an arrow for the sake of Allah, will have a reward equal to the emancipation of a slave.".
iwh_en: 'Amr bin 'Abasah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying: "He who shoots an arrow for the sake of Allah, will have a reward equal to the emancipation of a slave.".
iwh_id: 'Amr bin 'Abasah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan: I heard Rasulullah ﷺ saying: "He who shoots an arrow for the sake of Allah, will have a reward equal to the emancipation of seorang budak.".

ar:
وعن أبي يحيي خريم بن فاتك رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏
"‏من أنفق نفقة في سبيل الله كتب له سبعمائة ضعف‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Yahya Khuraim bin Fatik (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He who makes a contribution in Allah's way, will have his reward seven hundred times recorded to his credit.".
iwh_en: Abu Yahya Khuraim bin Fatik (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "He who makes a contribution in Allah's way, will have his reward seven hundred times recorded to his credit.".
iwh_id: Abu Yahya Khuraim bin Fatik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He who makes a contribution in Allah's way, will have his reward seven hundred times recorded to his credit.".

ar:
وعن أبي سعيد رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏
"‏ما من عبد يصوم يومًا في سبيل الله إلا باعد الله بذلك اليوم وجهه عن النار سبعين خريفًا‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Every slave of Allah who observes Saum (fasting) for a day in the Cause of Allah, Allah will keep his face from Hell-fire at a distance of seventy years.".
iwh_en: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Every slave of Allah who observes Saum (fasting) for a day in the Cause of Allah, Allah will keep his face from Hell-fire at a distance of seventy years.".
iwh_id: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Every slave of Allah who observes Saum (fasting) for a day in the Cause of Allah, Allah will menjaga his face from Hell-fire at a distance of seventy years.".

ar:

"‏من صام يومًا في سبيل الله جعل الله بينه وبين النار خندقًا كما بين السماء والأرض‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن صحيح‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He who dies without having fought in the Cause of Allah or without having thought of doing so, will die with one characteristic of hypocrisy in him.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "He who dies without having fought in the Cause of Allah or without having thought of doing so, will die with one characteristic of hypocrisy in him.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He who dies tanpa having fought in the Cause of Allah or tanpa having berpikir of doing jadi, will die with one characteristic of hypocrisy in him.".

ar:

"‏إن بالمدينة لرجالا ما سرتم مسيرًا، ولا قطعتم واديًا إلا كانوا معكم حبسهم المرض‏"‏

sunnah_ref_en: Jabir (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: We accompanied the Prophet (ﷺ) in an expedition when he (ﷺ) said, "Some people have remained behind us in Al-Madinah, and we never cross a valley but they are with us. They share the reward with us because they have been held back by valid excuse."In another narration the wordings are: "...by any genuine excuse."In another narration the wordings are: "They are your partners in reward.".

ar:
وعن أبي موسى، رضي الله عنه أن أعرابيا أتى النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم فقال‏:‏ يا رسول الله الرجل يقاتل للمغنم، والرجل يقاتل ليذكر، والرجل يقاتل ليرى مكانه‏؟‏ 
وفي رواية‏:‏ يقاتل شجاعة، ويقاتل حَمِيّة‏.‏
وفي رواية‏:‏ ويقاتل غضبًا، فمن في سبيل الله‏؟‏ فقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏
"‏من قاتل لتكون كلمة الله هي العليا، فهو في سبيل الله‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Musa (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A bedouin came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and said: "O Messenger of Allah! One man fights for booty, another fights to win fame, and the third fights for show off." Another narration is: "One fights for displaying his valour, another fights out of his family pride." Another narration is: "One fights out of rage." He asked: "Which of them is fighting in the Cause of Allah?" The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "The one who fights so that Word of Allah (Islam) be exalted, is the one who fights in the Cause of Allah.".
iwh_en: Abu Musa (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a bedouin came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: "O Messenger of Allah! One man fights for booty, another fights to win fame, and the third fights for show off." Another narration is: "One fights for displaying his valour, another fights out of his family pride." Another narration is: "One fights out of rage." He asked: "Which of them is fighting in the Cause of Allah?" the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "The one who fights so that Word of Allah (Islam) be exalted, is the one who fights in the Cause of Allah.".
iwh_id: Abu Musa (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa a bedouin datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: "O Rasulullah! One man fights for booty, another fights to win fame, and the third fights for menunjukkan off." Another narration is: "One fights for displaying his valour, another fights out of his family pride." Another narration is: "One fights out of rage." Ia bertanya: "Which of them is fighting di Cause of Allah?" Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "The one yang fights sehingga Word of Allah (Islam) be exalted, is the one yang fights di Cause of Allah.".

ar:

isnad_chain.name_en: 'Amr bin Al-'As

sunnah_ref_en: 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'As (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "A detachment of soldiers, large or small, who fights in the way of Allah, gets its share of booty and returns safe and sound, receives in advance two- thirds of its reward (only one-third remaining to its credit will be received in the Hereafter). And a troop of soldiers, large or small, that returns disappointed and is afflicted by misery, will receive its full reward (in the Hereafter).".
iwh_en: 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'As (may Allah be pleased with them) reported: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "A detachment of soldiers, large or small, who fights in the way of Allah, gets its share of booty and returns safe and sound, receives in advance two- thirds of its reward (only one-third remaining to its credit will be received in the Hereafter). And a troop of soldiers, large or small, that returns disappointed and is afflicted by misery, will receive its full reward (in the Hereafter).".
iwh_id: 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'As (radhiyallahu 'anhum) meriwayatkan: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "A detachment of soldiers, besar or kecil, who fights in the way of Allah, gets its share of booty dan returns safe dan sound, receives in advance two- thirds of its reward (only one-third remaining to its credit will be received di Akhirat). And a troop of soldiers, besar or kecil, that returns disappointed dan is afflicted by misery, will receive its full reward (di Akhirat).".

ar:
وعن أبي أمامة، رضي الله عنه أن رجلا قال‏:‏ يا رسول الله ائذن لي في السياحة‏.‏ فقال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏
"‏إن سياحة أمتي الجهاد في سبيل الله، عز وجل‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود بإسناد جيد‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Umamah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A man sought permission from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) to travel in the land. He (ﷺ) said to him, "Travel for my people is Jihad in the Cause of Allah, glory be to Him.".
iwh_en: Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a man sought permission from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ to travel in the land. He (ﷺ) said to him, "Travel for my people is Jihad in the Cause of Allah, glory be to Him.".
iwh_id: Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa seorang laki-laki mencari permission from Rasulullah ﷺ to travel in the land. He (ﷺ) bersabda to him, "Travel for my orang-orang is Jihad in the Cause of Allah, glory be to Him.".

isnad_chain.name_en: 'Amr bin Al-'As

sunnah_ref_en: 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'As (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The return from an expedition is an act as meritorious as fighting.".
iwh_en: 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'As (may Allah be pleased with them) reported: the Prophet ﷺ said, "The return from an expedition is an act as meritorious as fighting.".
iwh_id:
'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'As (radhiyallahu 'anhum) meriwayatkan:
Nabi ﷺ berkata, "The return from an expedition is an act as meritorious as fighting.".

ar:
وعن السائب بن يزيد، رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ لما قدم النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم من غزوة تبوك تلقاه الناس، فلقيته مع الصبيان على ثنية الوداع‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: As-Saib bin Yazid

sunnah_ref_en: As-Sa'ib bin Yazid (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: When the Prophet (ﷺ) returned from the battle of Tabuk, people went out from Al-Madinah to meet him and I also met him with other children at Thaniyah-tul-Wada'..
iwh_en: As-Sa'ib bin Yazid (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that when the Prophet ﷺ returned from the battle of Tabuk, people went out from Al-Madinah to meet him and I also met him with other children at Thaniyah-tul-Wada'..
iwh_id: As-Sa'ib bin Yazid (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa ketika Nabi ﷺ returned from the battle of Tabuk, orang-orang went out from Al-Madinah to meet him dan aku juga met him with other children at Thaniyah-tul-Wada'..

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Umamah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "He who neither takes part in fighting nor equips a warrior nor looks after his (the warrior's) family, will be afflicted by severe calamities before the Day of Resurrection.".
iwh_en: Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Prophet ﷺ said, "He who neither takes part in fighting nor equips a warrior nor looks after his (the warrior's) family, will be afflicted by severe calamities before the Day of Resurrection.".
iwh_id: Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata, "Barangsiapa yang neither takes part in fighting dan tidak equips a warrior dan tidak looks setelah his (the warrior's) family, will be afflicted by severe calamities sebelum Hari Kebangkitan.".

sunnah_ref_en: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Fight the polytheists with your wealth, lives and tongues.".

ar: وعن أبي عمرو ويقال‏:‏ أبو حكيم النعمان بن مقرن رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ شهدت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم إذا لم يقاتل من أول النهار أخر القتال حتى تزول الشمس وتهب الريح وينزل النصر‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود، والترمذي، وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن صحيح‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: An-Nu'man bin Muqarrin (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I was with the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) when I witnessed that if he did not begin fighting in the early part of the day, he would postpone fighting till the sun had declined, the blowing of the breeze had blown and the victory from Allah had come..

iwh_id: An-Nu'man bin Muqarrin (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa i was with Rasulullah ﷺ when I witnessed that jika ia tidak begin fighting di early part dari day, ia postpone fighting till the sun had declined, the blowing dari breeze had blown dan the victory from Allah had come..

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Do not wish for an encounter with the enemy. Pray to Allah to grant you safety; (but) when you encounter them, show patience.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Do not wish for an encounter with the enemy. Pray to Allah to grant you safety; (but) when you encounter them, show patience.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Jangan wish for an encounter with the enemy. Pray to Allah to grant you safety; (namun) when you encounter them, menunjukkan patience.".

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en:
Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "The martyrs are of five kinds: One who dies of plague; one who dies of disease of his belly; the drowned; one who dies under the debris (of construction, etc.), and one who dies while fighting in the way of Allah."

.
iwh_en:

.
iwh_id:
Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "The martyrs are of five kinds: One who dies of plague; one who dies of disease of his belly; the drowned; one who dies under the debris (of construction, etc.), and one who dies while fighting in the way of Allah."

ar: وعنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏"‏ما تعدون الشهداء فيكم‏؟‏ قالوا‏:‏ يا رسول الله من قتل في سبيل الله فهو شهيد‏.‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏إن شهداء أمتي إذًا لقليل‏!‏‏"‏ قالوا‏:‏ فمن يا رسول الله ‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏من قتل في سبيل الله فهو شهيد، ومن مات في سبيل الله فهو شهيد، ومن مات في الطاعون فهو شهيد، ومن مات في البطن فهو شهيد، والغريق شهيد‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Whom do you reckon to be martyr amongst you?" The Companions replied: "The one who is killed in Allah's way." He said, "In that case, the martyrs among my people would be few." The Companions asked: "O Messenger of Allah! Then who are the martyrs?" He replied, "He who is killed in the way of Allah is a martyr; he who dies naturally in the Cause of Allah is a martyr; he who dies of plague is a martyr; and he who dies of a belly disease is a martyr; and he who is drowned is a martyr.".This Hadith shows the care Allah has for this Ummah, which is the best Ummah of mankind. (Editor's Note)
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Whom do you reckon to be martyr amongst you?" The Companions replied: "The one who is killed in Allah's way." He said, "In that case, the martyrs among my people would be few." The Companions asked: "O Messenger of Allah! Then who are the martyrs?" He replied, "He who is killed in the way of Allah is a martyr; he who dies naturally in the Cause of Allah is a martyr; he who dies of plague is a martyr; and he who dies of a belly disease is a martyr; and he who is drowned is a martyr.".This Hadith shows the care Allah has for this Ummah, which is the best Ummah of mankind. (Editor's Note)
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Whom do you reckon to be martyr amongst you?" The Companions menjawab: "The one who is killed in Allah's way." He bersabda, "In that case, the martyrs di antara my orang-orang would be few." The Companions bertanya: "O Rasulullah! Kemudian who are the martyrs?" Ia menjawab, "He who is killed in the way of Allah is a martyr; he who dies naturally in the Cause of Allah is a martyr; he who dies of plague is a martyr; dan ia who dies of a belly disease is a martyr; dan ia who is drowned is a martyr.".This Hadith shows the care Allah has for this Ummah, which is the best Ummah of mankind. (Editor's Note)

isnad_chain.name_en: 'Amr bin Al-'As

sunnah_ref_en: 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'As (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He who is killed while defending his property is a martyr.".
iwh_en: 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'As (may Allah be pleased with them) reported: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "He who is killed while defending his property is a martyr.".
iwh_id: 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'As (radhiyallahu 'anhum) meriwayatkan: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He who is killed while defending his property is a martyr.".

ar:
وعن أبي الأعور سعيد بن زيد بن عمرو بن نفيل، أحد العشرة المشهود لهم بالجنة، رضي الله عنهم، قال‏:‏ سمعت رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقول‏:‏ ‏
"‏من قتل دون ماله فهو شهيد، ومن قتل دون دمه فهو شهيد، ومن قتل دون دينه فهو شهيد، ومن قتل دون عرضه فهو شهيد‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن صحيح‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Sa'id bin Zaid bin 'Amr bin Nufail (May Allah be pleased with him) reported, one of the ten Companions who were given the glad tidings of entering Jannah reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saying: "He who dies while defending his property is a martyr; he who dies in defence of his own life is a martyr; and he who dies on defense of his faith is a martyr, he who dies in defence of his family is a martyr.".
iwh_en: Sa'id bin Zaid bin 'Amr bin Nufail (may Allah be pleased with him) reported, one of the ten Companions who were given the glad tidings of entering Jannah reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saying: "He who dies while defending his property is a martyr; he who dies in defence of his own life is a martyr; and he who dies on defense of his faith is a martyr, he who dies in defence of his family is a martyr.".
iwh_id: Sa'id bin Zaid bin 'Amr bin Nufail (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan, one of the ten Companions who were given the glad tidings of entering Jannah meriwayatkan: Saya mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ saying: "Barangsiapa yang dies while defending his property is a martyr; barangsiapa yang dies in defence of his own life is a martyr; dan barangsiapa yang dies on defense of his iman is a martyr, barangsiapa yang dies in defence of his family is a martyr.".

matn_ar_clean: وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ جاء رجل إلى رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم فقال‏:‏ يا رسول الله أرأيت إن جاء رجل يريد أخذ مالي‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏فلا تعطه مالك‏"‏ قال‏:‏ أرأيت إن قاتلني‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏قاتله‏"‏ قال‏:‏ أرأيت إن قتلني قال‏:‏ ‏"‏فأنت شهيد‏"‏ قال‏:‏ أرأيت إن قتلته‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏هو في النار‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A man came to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and asked, "O Messenger of Allah! What shall I do if someone comes to me with the intention of taking away my property?" He replied, "Do not hand over it to him." The man asked, "What shall I do if he fights me?" The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Then fight him." "What will be my position in the Hereafter if he has killed me?" The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) replied, "In that case you are a martyr." The man asked: "What if I killed him?" The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) replied, "He will be in the Hell- fire.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a man came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and asked, "O Messenger of Allah! What shall I do if someone comes to me with the intention of taking away my property?" He replied, "Do not hand over it to him." The man asked, "What shall I do if he fights me?" the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Then fight him." "What will be my position in the Hereafter if he has killed me?" the Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied, "In that case you are a martyr." The man asked: "What if I killed him?" the Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied, "He will be in the Hell- fire.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa seorang laki-laki came to Rasulullah ﷺ dan bertanya, "O Rasulullah! What shall I do if someone comes to me with the intention of taking away my property?" Ia menjawab, "Jangan hand over it to him." The man bertanya, "What shall I do if he fights me?" Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Kemudian fight him." "What will be my position di Akhirat if he has killed me?" Rasulullah ﷺ menjawab, "In that case you are a martyr." The man bertanya: "What if I killed him?" Rasulullah ﷺ menjawab, "He will be in the Hell- fire.".

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He who sets free a Muslim slave, Allah will deliver from the fire of Hell every limb of his body in return for every limb of the slave's body, even his private parts.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "He who sets free a Muslim slave, Allah will deliver from the fire of Hell every limb of his body in return for every limb of the slave's body, even his private parts.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He who sets free a Muslim slave, Allah will deliver from the fire of Hell every limb of his body in return for every limb of the slave's body, even his private parts.".

ar: وعن أبي ذر رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قلت يا رسول الله أي الأعمال أفضل‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏الإيمان بالله، والجهاد في سبيل الله‏"‏ قلت‏:‏ أي الرقاب أفضل‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ‏"‏أنفسها عند أهلها وأكثرها ثمنًا” ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏
ar_clean: وعن أبي ذر رضي الله عنه قال: قلت يا رسول الله أي الأعمال أفضل؟ قال: "الإيمان بالله، والجهاد في سبيل الله" قلت: أي الرقاب أفضل؟ قال: "أنفسها عند أهلها وأكثرها ثمنًا” ((متفق عليه))

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Dharr (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I asked the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ), "Which deed is most excellent?" He replied, "Faith in Allah and Jihad in His path." I then asked, "Which slaves are most excellent (to set free)?" He replied, "Those who are held in high esteem by their people and whose value is higher.".
iwh_en: Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that i asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, "Which deed is most excellent?" He replied, "Faith in Allah and Jihad in His path." I then asked, "Which slaves are most excellent (to set free)?" He replied, "Those who are held in high esteem by their people and whose value is higher.".
iwh_id: Abu Dharr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa i bertanya Rasulullah ﷺ, "Which deed is most excellent?" Ia menjawab, "Faith in Allah and Jihad in His path." I kemudian bertanya, "Which slaves are most excellent (to set free)?" Ia menjawab, "Those who are held in tinggi esteem by their orang-orang dan whose value is higher.".

وعن المعرور بن سويد قال‏:‏ رأيت أبا ذر رضي الله عنه وعليه حلة وعلى غلامه مثلها، فسألته عن ذلك فذكر أنه ساب رجلا على عهد رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم فعيره بأمه فقال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏
"‏إنك امرؤ فيك جاهلية‏"‏ ‏:‏ هم إخوانكم، وخولكم جعلهم الله تحت أيديكم فمن كان أخوه تحت يده فليطعمه مما يأكل ويلبسه مما يلبس ولا تكلفوهم ما يغلبهم، فإن كلفتموهم فأعينوهم” ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: Al-Ma'rur bin Suwaid (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: I saw Abu Dharr (May Allah be pleased with him) wearing a nice gown, and his slave was also wearing one similar to it. I asked him about it, and he said that he had exchanged harsh words with a person during the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and put him to shame by making a reference to his mother. That person came to the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and made mention of that to him. Thereupon the Messenger of Allah said, "You are a person who has remnants of the 'Days of Ignorance' in you. Your slaves are your brothers. Allah has placed them under your authority. He who has his brother under him, should feed him from whatever he eats, and dress him with whatever he wears, and do not burden them (assign burdensome task to them) beyond their capacity; and if you burden them then help them.".
iwh_en: Al-Ma'rur bin Suwaid (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that i saw Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) wearing a nice gown, and his slave was also wearing one similar to it. I asked him about it, and he said that he had exchanged harsh words with a person during the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and put him to shame by making a reference to his mother. That person came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and made mention of that to him. Thereupon the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "You are a person who has remnants of the 'Days of Ignorance' in you. Your slaves are your brothers. Allah has placed them under your authority. He who has his brother under him, should feed him from whatever he eats, and dress him with whatever he wears, and do not burden them (assign burdensome task to them) beyond their capacity; and if you burden them then help them.".
iwh_id: Al-Ma'rur bin Suwaid (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa i saw Abu Dharr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) mengenakan a nice gown, and his slave was juga mengenakan one similar to it. Saya bertanya him tentang it, dan ia bersabda bahwa he had exchanged harsh words with seseorang selama the lifetime of Rasulullah ﷺ dan meletakkan him to shame by making a reference to ibunya. That person came to Rasulullah ﷺ dan membuat menyebut of that to him. Thereupon Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "You are seseorang who has remnants of the 'Days of Ignorance' in you. Your slaves are your brothers. Allah has placed them under your authority. He who has saudaranya under him, should feed him from whatever he eats, and dress him with whatever he wears, and jangan burden them (assign burdensome task to them) beyond their capacity; dan if you burden them kemudian help them.".

ar: وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه عن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏إذا أتى أحدكم خادمه بطعامه، فإن لم يجلسه معه فليناوله لقمة أو لقمتين أو أُكلة أو أُكلتين فإنه ولي علاجه‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه البخاري‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏ (9)

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "When your servant brings food for you and you do not seat him with you, you should at least give him a morsel or two out of it because he has prepared it himself.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "When your servant brings food for you and you do not seat him with you, you should at least give him a morsel or two out of it because he has prepared it himself.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "When your servant brings food for you dan you jangan seat him with you, you should at least memberikan him a morsel or two out of it because he has prepared it himself.".

"‏إن العبد إذا نصح لسيده، وأحسن عبادة الله، فله أجره مرتين‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

Ibn 'Umar (May Allah be pleased with them) reported: I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) saying, "When a slave is sincere to his master and worships Allah well, He will have a double reward."

.

.

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "The faithful and diligent slave will have a double reward." (Abu Hurairah added:) By Him in Whose Hand the soul of Abu Hurairah is! but for Jihad in the Cause of Allah, and Hajj and kindness to my mother, I would have preferred to die as a slave..
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "The faithful and diligent slave will have a double reward." (Abu Hurairah added:) By Him in Whose Hand the soul of Abu Hurairah is! but for Jihad in the Cause of Allah, and Hajj and kindness to my mother, I would have preferred to die as a slave..
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "The faithful dan diligent slave will have a double reward." (Abu Hurairah added:) By Him in Whose Hand the soul of Abu Hurairah is! namun for Jihad in the Cause of Allah, dan Hajj dan kindness to my mother, I would have preferred to die as seorang budak..

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Burda's father

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "A slave who worships his Rubb (Allah) well and discharges efficiently and faithfully the duties which are assigned to him by his master, will have a double reward.".
iwh_en: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "A slave who worships his Rubb (Allah) well and discharges efficiently and faithfully the duties which are assigned to him by his master, will have a double reward.".
iwh_id: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "A slave who worships his Rubb (Allah) well dan discharges efficiently dan faithfully the duties which are assigned to him by his master, will have a double reward.".

ar:
وعنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏
"‏ثلاثة لهم أجران‏:‏ رجل من أهل الكتاب آمن بنبيه، وآمن بمحمد، والعبد المملوك إذا أدى حق الله، وحق مواليه، ورجل كانت له أمة فأدبها فأحسن تأديبها وعلمها فأحسن تعليمها، ثم أعتقها فتزوجها فله أجران‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

وعنه قال: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم :

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "Three kinds of people will have a double reward: A man from the People of the Book who believes in his Prophet and (also) believes in Muhammad; a slave who discharges properly the duties towards Allah and towards his master; and a man who possesses a slave-girl and teaches her manners, educates her well, and frees her and then marries her.".
iwh_en: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Three kinds of people will have a double reward: A man from the People of the Book who believes in his Prophet and (also) believes in Muhammad; a slave who discharges properly the duties towards Allah and towards his master; and a man who possesses a slave-girl and teaches her manners, educates her well, and frees her and then marries her.".
iwh_id: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Three kinds of orang-orang will have a double reward: A man from the People of the Book who believes in his Prophet and (juga) believes in Muhammad; seorang budak who discharges properly the duties kepada Allah dan kepada his master; dan seorang laki-laki who possesses seorang budak-girl dan teaches her manners, educates her well, and frees her dan kemudian marries her.".

isnad_chain.name_en: Ma'qil bin Yasar

sunnah_ref_en:
Ma'qil bin Yasar (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "The reward of worship performed at a time of trials is equal in reward to an emigration to me."

.

ar: وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه أن رجلا أتى النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم يتقاضاه فأغلظ له فهم به أصحابه، فقال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏"‏دعوه فإن لصاحب الحق مقالا‏"‏ ثم قال‏:‏ ‏"‏أعطوه سنا مثل سنه‏"‏ قالوا‏:‏ يا رسول الله لا نجد إلا أمثل من سنه، قال‏:‏ ‏"‏أعطوه فإن خيركم أحسنكم قضاء‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏ ‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: A man demanded of the Prophet (ﷺ) for repayment of a loan and was harsh to him. His Companions were about to attack him, but he (ﷺ) said, "Leave him, as the creditor is entitled to make a demand. Give him a camel of the same age as the camel that is due to him." They said: "We find a better camel senior to it in age." He (ﷺ) said, "Then buy it and give it to him; verily the best of you is the one who is the best in discharging his obligations (repayment of loans).".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that a man demanded of the Prophet ﷺ for repayment of a loan and was harsh to him. His Companions were about to attack him, but he (ﷺ) said, "Leave him, as the creditor is entitled to make a demand. Give him a camel of the same age as the camel that is due to him." They said: "We find a better camel senior to it in age." He (ﷺ) said, "Then buy it and give it to him; verily the best of you is the one who is the best in discharging his obligations (repayment of loans).".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa seorang laki-laki demanded of Nabi ﷺ for repayment of a loan dan was harsh to him. His Companions were tentang to attack him, namun ia (ﷺ) berkata, "Leave him, as the creditor is entitled to membuat a demand. Give him a camel of the same age as the camel that is due to him." They berkata: "We menemukan a better camel senior to it in age." He (ﷺ) berkata, "Kemudian buy it dan memberikan it to him; verily the best of you is the one who is the best in discharging his obligations (repayment of loans).".

sunnah_ref_en: Jabir (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "May Allah show mercy to a man who adopts a kind attitude when he sells, buys and demands for the repayment of loans.".
iwh_en: Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "May Allah show mercy to a man who adopts a kind attitude when he sells, buys and demands for the repayment of loans.".

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Qatadah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He who likes Allah to deliver him from the calamities of the Day of Resurrection, let him either give respite to a debtor or grant him remission (of loans) in straitened circumstances.".

iwh_id: Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He who likes Allah to deliver him from the calamities of the Hari Kebangkitan, let him either memberikan respite to a debtor or grant him remission (of loans) in straitened circumstances.".

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "There was a person who used to loan money to the people and he used to say to his servant: 'When an insolvent person comes to you, show him leniency so that Allah may forbear our faults.' So when he met Allah (i.e., when he died), Allah forgave him.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "There was a person who used to loan money to the people and he used to say to his servant: 'When an insolvent person comes to you, show him leniency so that Allah may forbear our faults.' So when he met Allah (i.e., when he died), Allah forgave him.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berkata, "There was seseorang yang biasa loan money kepada orang-orang dan ia biasa berkata to his servant: 'When an insolvent person comes to you, menunjukkan him leniency sehingga Allah may forbear our faults.' Jadi ketika ia met Allah (i.e., ketika ia died), Allah forgave him.".

ar: وعن أبي مسعود البدري رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏"‏حوسب رجل ممن كان قبلكم فلم يوجد له من الخير شيء إلا أنه كان يخالط الناس، وكان موسرًا وكان يأمر غلمانه أن يتجاوزوا عن المعسر قال الله عز وجل‏:‏ ‏"‏نحن أحق بذلك منه، تجاوزوا عنه‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏
ar_clean: وعن أبي مسعود البدري رضي الله عنه قال: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم : "حوسب رجل ممن كان قبلكم فلم يوجد له من الخير شيء إلا أنه كان يخالط الناس، وكان موسرًا وكان يأمر غلمانه أن يتجاوزوا عن المعسر قال الله عز وجل: "نحن أحق بذلك منه، تجاوزوا عنه" ((رواه مسلم)).

matn_ar: وكان موسرًا وكان يأمر غلمانه أن يتجاوزوا عن المعسر قال الله عز وجل: "نحن أحق بذلك منه، تجاوزوا عنه" ((رواه مسلم)).
matn_ar_clean: وكان موسرا وكان يأمر غلمانه أن يتجاوزوا عن المعسر قال الله عز وجل: "نحن أحق بذلك منه، تجاوزوا عنه" ((رواه مسلم)).
sunnah_ref_en: Abu Mas'ud Al-Badri (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "A person from amongst the people who lived before you was called to account by Allah on the Day of Resurrection. No good deeds were found in his credit except that he being a rich man had (financial) dealings with people and had commanded his servants to show leniency to those who were in straitened circumstances. Upon this Allah, the Exalted, and Majestic said: 'I am more entitled to this attribute, so waive (his faults)."'.
iwh_en: Abu Mas'ud Al-Badri (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "A person from amongst the people who lived before you was called to account by Allah on the Day of Resurrection. No good deeds were found in his credit except that he being a rich man had (financial) dealings with people and had commanded his servants to show leniency to those who were in straitened circumstances. Upon this Allah, the Exalted, and Majestic said: 'I am more entitled to this attribute, so waive (his faults)."'.
iwh_id: Abu Mas'ud Al-Badri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "A person from amongst the orang-orang who lived sebelum you was called to account by Allah di Hari Kebangkitan. No baik deeds were menemukan in his credit except that he being a rich man had (financial) dealings with orang-orang dan had memerintahkan his servants to menunjukkan leniency to those who were in straitened circumstances. Upon this Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala, and Majestic bersabda: 'I am more entitled to this attribute, jadi waive (his faults)."'.

وعن حذيفة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ أتي الله تعالى بعبد من عباده آتاه الله مالا فقال له ماذا عملت في الدنيا‏؟‏ قال‏:‏ ولا يكتمون الله حديثا- قال‏:‏ يا رب آتيتني مالك فكنت أبايع الناس، وكان من خلقي الجواز فكنت أتيسر على الموسر وأنظر المعسر فقال الله تعالى‏:‏ “‏
"‏أنا أحق بذا منك، تجاوزوا عن عبدي‏"‏ فقال عقبة بن عامر، وأبو مسعود الأنصاري رضي الله عنهما هكذا سمعناه من في رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه مسلم‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

matn_ar_clean:

"أنا أحق بذا منك، تجاوزوا عن عبدي" فقال عقبة بن عامر، وأبو مسعود الأنصاري رضي الله عنهما هكذا سمعناه من في رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم. ((رواه مسلم)).
sunnah_ref_en: Hudhaifah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "A slave of Allah whom He had endowed with wealth, will be brought before Him. Allah will ask him: 'What did you do in the world?' (They cannot conceal anything from Allah.) He will say: 'O my Rubb, You endowed me with Your wealth; I used to enter into transactions with people and it was my nature to be lenient to the insolvent ones. I used to give respite to those who were in straitened circumstances.' Whereupon Allah will say: 'I am more entitled than you to do this. So forgive my slave'." 'Uqbah bin 'Amir and Abu Mas'ud Al- Ansari (May Allah be pleased with them) said, "Thus we heard it from the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ).".
iwh_en: Hudhaifah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "A slave of Allah whom He had endowed with wealth, will be brought before Him. Allah will ask him: 'What did you do in the world?' (They cannot conceal anything from Allah.) He will say: 'O my Rubb, You endowed me with Your wealth; I used to enter into transactions with people and it was my nature to be lenient to the insolvent ones. I used to give respite to those who were in straitened circumstances.' Whereupon Allah will say: 'I am more entitled than you to do this. So forgive my slave'." 'Uqbah bin 'Amir and Abu Mas'ud Al- Ansari (may Allah be pleased with them) said, "Thus we heard it from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.".
iwh_id: Hudhaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "A slave of Allah whom He had dianugerahi wealth, will be brought sebelum Him. Allah will ask him: 'What did you do di dunia?' (They cannot conceal anything from Allah.) He will say: 'O my Rubb, You endowed me with Your wealth; I biasa masuk ke into transactions with orang-orang dan it was my nature to be lenient to the insolvent ones. I biasa memberikan respite to those who were in straitened circumstances.' Whereupon Allah will say: 'I am more entitled than you to do this. Jadi forgive my slave'." 'Uqbah bin 'Amir and Abu Mas'ud Al- Ansari (radhiyallahu 'anhum) bersabda, "Thus we heard it from Rasulullah ﷺ.".

ar:
وعن أبي هريرة رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏:‏ ‏
"‏من أنظر معسرًا أو وضع له، أظله الله يوم القيامة تحت ظل عرشه يوم لا ظل إلا ظله‏"‏‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه الترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن صحيح‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

isnad_chain.name_en: Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, "He who gives respite to someone who is in straitened circumstances, or grants him remission, Allah will shelter him in the shade of His Throne, on the Day of Resurrection, when there will be no shade except its shade.".
iwh_en: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "He who gives respite to someone who is in straitened circumstances, or grants him remission, Allah will shelter him in the shade of His Throne, on the Day of Resurrection, when there will be no shade except its shade.".
iwh_id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "He who gives respite to someone who is in straitened circumstances, or grants him remission, Allah will shelter him in the shade of His Throne, di Hari Kebangkitan, when there will be no shade except its shade.".

ar: وعن جابر، رضي الله عنه أن النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم اشترى منه بعيرًا فوزن له فأرجح‏.‏ ‏(‏‏(‏متفق عليه‏)‏‏)‏

sunnah_ref_en: Jabir (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet (ﷺ) purchased a camel from me and weighed more than its price..
iwh_en: Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Prophet ﷺ purchased a camel from me and weighed more than its price..
iwh_id: Jabir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ purchased a camel from me dan weighed more than its price..

ar:
وعن أبي صفوان سويد بن قيس، رضي الله عنه قال‏:‏ جلبت أنا ومخرمة العبدي بزًا من هجر، فجاءنا النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم فساومنا سراويل، وعندي وزان يزن بالأجر، فقال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم للوزان ‏
"‏زن وأرجح‏"‏ ‏(‏‏(‏رواه أبو داود والترمذي وقال‏:‏ حديث حسن صحيح‏)‏‏)‏‏.‏

sunnah_ref_en: Abu Safwan Suwaid bin Qais (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: Makhramah Al-'Abdi and I procured some drapery from Hajar and brought them to Makkah. The Prophet (ﷺ) came and bargained with us for some trousers and we sold them to him. We had a person who weighed the cloth in order to fix the price. The Prophet (ﷺ) said to him, "Weigh and add a little to it.".
iwh_en: Abu Safwan Suwaid bin Qais (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that makhramah Al-'Abdi and I procured some drapery from Hajar and brought them to Makkah. the Prophet ﷺ came and bargained with us for some trousers and we sold them to him. We had a person who weighed the cloth in order to fix the price. the Prophet ﷺ said to him, "Weigh and add a little to it.".
iwh_id: Abu Safwan Suwaid bin Qais (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa makhramah Al-'Abdi dan aku procured some drapery from Hajar dan membawa them to Makkah. Nabi ﷺ came dan bargained bersama kami for some trousers dan kami sold them to him. We had seseorang who weighed the cloth untuk fix the price. Nabi ﷺ bersabda kepadanya, "Weigh dan add a little to it.".
